1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Build the GAIN investigation database from the raw challenge corpus.
3	
4	Parses three datasets (layout per the challenge data manual) into one DuckDB file:
5	
6	  <data-root>/congress_press/   JSONL press releases (year dirs + 2026 files at root)
7	  <data-root>/senate/           Senate LDA filings + contributions (JSON arrays, streamed)
8	  <data-root>/house/            House LDA registrations + quarterlies (one XML per filing)
9	
10	Guarantees:
11	  * Every row carries a raw-record pointer (src_file + src_line/src_index, or the XML
12	    path) so any query result resolves to a citable raw record in one step
13	    (see show_record.py).
14	  * A sanity report reconciles row counts against the data manual's published 2025
15	    scale before the database is trusted. Written next to the DB as sanity_report.md.
16	
17	Usage:
18	  python build_db.py --data-root data/                       # everything present
19	  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --years 2025 2026     # pilot slice
20	  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --sample 2025-Q1      # smoke mode (minutes)
21	
22	Caveat: Senate JSON key names and House XML tag names were drafted from the data
23	manual before the raw data landed and are coded defensively (multiple candidate
24	keys/tags, strip everything). The sanity report is the tripwire for mismatches —
25	verify against real data before trusting downstream queries.
26	"""
27	
28	import argparse
29	import json
30	import re
31	import sys
32	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	import duckdb
36	import ijson
37	
38	BATCH = 50000
39	
40	# Sanity report contains non-ASCII; Windows pipes default to cp1252.
41	if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
42	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")
43	
44	# Data manual's published 2025 scale, used by the sanity report.
45	MANUAL_2025 = {
46	    "press_releases": 48000,        # "~48K releases in 2025"
47	    "senate_filings": 108225,
48	    "senate_contributions": 39438,
49	    "house_filings": 108522,        # ~7,762 registrations + ~100,760 quarterlies
50	}
51	
52	# Bill citations: longest alternatives first so "H.J.Res." doesn't match as "H.R.".
53	BILL_RE = re.compile(
54	    r"\b("
55	    r"H\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|"
56	    r"H\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|"
57	    r"H\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*RES\.?|"
58	    r"H\.?\s*R\.?|S\.?"
59	    r")\s*(\d{1,5})\b",
60	    re.IGNORECASE,
61	)
62	
63	
64	def norm_bill(prefix: str, number: str) -> str:
65	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Z]", "", prefix.upper()) + number
66	
67	
68	def extract_bills(text):
69	    if not text:
70	        return
71	    for m in BILL_RE.finditer(text):
72	        yield norm_bill(m.group(1), m.group(2)), m.group(0)
73	
74	
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------- press issue tagging
76	#
77	# Tags press-release text with ALI issue codes (data/senate/constants/
78	# lobbying_activity_issues.json) using a curated keyword vocabulary, so press
79	# volume on an issue can be compared to lobbying spend on the same code
80	# (v_spend_by_issue_quarter). This is the press-side analogue of extract_bills:
81	# one raw-record-pointer'd row per (issue_code, keyword) that appears in a release.
82	#
83	# DESIGN / PROVENANCE OF THE MAPPING (read before trusting any downstream count):
84	#   * Precision over recall. Keywords are distinctive phrases, not bare topic words
85	#     ("border security" not "border"; "national defense" not "defense"), matched
86	#     on whole-word boundaries, case-insensitively, with flexible interior
87	#     whitespace (so "health\ncare" across a line break still matches "health care").
88	#   * Each keyword string maps to exactly ONE code (asserted at build time), so a
89	#     hit is unambiguously attributable. Where a concept could belong to two codes,
90	#     it is assigned to the closest ALI code and flagged in SHAKY_MAPPINGS below.
91	#   * NOT exhaustive over the ~79 ALI codes. It covers the codes congressional
92	#     press releases actually discuss, with vocabulary specific enough to trust.
93	#   * A "mention" is deduped per release: one row per (pr_id, issue_code, keyword),
94	#     never one-per-occurrence, so a release repeating "health care" ten times does
95	#     not outweigh ten releases mentioning it once. Volume metrics downstream should
96	#     COUNT(DISTINCT pr_id).
97	#
98	# Known ambiguities are recorded here (not hidden) and in the coupling write-up:
99	SHAKY_MAPPINGS = """
100	  tariff/tariffs -> TRD (Trade), NOT TAR: ALI's TAR code is narrowly "miscellaneous
101	    tariff bills"; LDA filers file broad tariff/trade-war policy under TRD, so press
102	    "tariffs" is mapped to TRD to keep the say-vs-pay join meaningful. Watch this one.
103	  health cluster HCR/MED/MMM/PHA/ALC bleeds: ACA "tax credits" tag BOTH HCR and TAX;
104	    "prescription drug" -> PHA but reads as HCR; medicare/medicaid -> MMM; opioid/
105	    fentanyl -> ALC though often framed as LAW or HCR.
106	  tech cluster CPI/SCI/TEC: "artificial intelligence" -> SCI, "semiconductor" -> CPI,
107	    "broadband/spectrum" -> TEC. Real releases blur these; treat the three as one
108	    loose "tech" bucket when reading trends.
109	  social security -> RET (no dedicated SS code exists; RET=Retirement is closest;
110	    it is arguably WEL). federal reserve -> BAN (could be MON). background check ->
111	    FIR (assumes firearms context; could be employment). insurance premiums are NOT
112	    mapped to INS (they are overwhelmingly health-context) -> INS is flood/auto/
113	    property only and is a weak, low-volume code. COM/SPO/GAM/ANI are low-precision
114	    breadth codes, included but noisy.
115	"""
116	
117	# code -> list of distinctive keyword phrases (lowercase, single-spaced).
118	ISSUE_KEYWORDS = {
119	    "HCR": ["health care", "healthcare", "affordable care act", "obamacare",
120	            "health insurance", "health coverage", "health care costs",
121	            "health care system", "public health", "health care coverage"],
122	    "MMM": ["medicare", "medicaid", "medicare advantage", "dual eligible"],
123	    "PHA": ["prescription drug", "prescription drugs", "drug prices", "drug pricing",
124	            "pharmacy", "pharmacist", "pharmacy benefit", "insulin"],
125	    "MED": ["medical research", "clinical trial", "clinical trials",
126	            "disease research", "national institutes of health", "biomedical research"],
127	    "TAX": ["tax credit", "tax credits", "tax cut", "tax cuts", "tax reform",
128	            "tax code", "taxation", "internal revenue service", "income tax",
129	            "corporate tax", "child tax credit", "estate tax"],
130	    "BUD": ["appropriations", "government shutdown", "debt ceiling", "federal budget",
131	            "budget deficit", "continuing resolution", "national debt", "debt limit",
132	            "discretionary spending"],
133	    "IMM": ["immigration", "immigrant", "immigrants", "border security",
134	            "southern border", "undocumented", "asylum seekers", "daca",
135	            "deportation", "border crossing", "illegal immigration"],
136	    "DEF": ["national defense", "defense department", "department of defense",
137	            "pentagon", "ndaa", "national defense authorization", "armed forces",
138	            "military readiness", "defense spending", "servicemembers"],
139	    "FIR": ["gun violence", "gun control", "gun safety", "firearm", "firearms",
140	            "second amendment", "assault weapons", "background check",
141	            "background checks", "ghost guns"],
142	    "ENG": ["clean energy", "renewable energy", "nuclear power", "energy policy",
143	            "energy prices", "power grid", "electric grid", "solar energy",
144	            "wind energy", "energy independence", "energy costs"],
145	    "ENV": ["climate change", "environmental protection", "greenhouse gas",
146	            "greenhouse gases", "carbon emissions", "superfund", "air pollution",
147	            "climate crisis", "environmental protection agency"],
148	    "CAW": ["clean water", "clean air", "drinking water", "air quality",
149	            "water quality", "pfas", "safe drinking water"],
150	    "FUE": ["gas prices", "gasoline prices", "oil and gas", "crude oil",
151	            "offshore drilling", "natural gas", "fossil fuels"],
152	    "AGR": ["farm bill", "farmers", "agriculture", "agricultural", "crop insurance",
153	            "department of agriculture", "livestock"],
154	    "VET": ["veterans", "veteran", "veterans affairs", "department of veterans affairs",
155	            "gi bill", "va health care", "veterans benefits"],
156	    "EDU": ["student loan", "student loans", "student debt", "public schools",
157	            "higher education", "department of education", "pell grant",
158	            "pell grants", "k-12", "school funding", "college affordability", "teachers"],
159	    "LBR": ["labor union", "labor unions", "minimum wage", "workers rights",
160	            "collective bargaining", "overtime pay", "workplace safety",
161	            "right to work", "project labor agreement", "antitrust", "unionize"],
162	    "HOU": ["affordable housing", "homelessness", "public housing", "housing costs",
163	            "housing affordability", "homeless", "section 8"],
164	    "RET": ["social security", "retirement savings", "pension", "pensions", "401(k)",
165	            "retirement security"],
166	    "CIV": ["civil rights", "voting rights", "civil liberties", "voter suppression",
167	            "lgbtq", "discrimination"],
168	    "FAM": ["abortion", "reproductive rights", "pro-life", "pro-choice",
169	            "paid family leave", "planned parenthood"],
170	    "POS": ["postal service", "usps", "post office", "postal facilities",
171	            "letter carriers"],
172	    "FIN": ["wall street", "securities and exchange", "cryptocurrency", "crypto",
173	            "stablecoin", "digital assets", "private equity", "hedge fund",
174	            "stock market", "sec regulation"],
175	    "BAN": ["banking", "banks", "community banks", "credit union", "credit unions",
176	            "federal reserve", "bank regulation"],
177	    "INS": ["flood insurance", "auto insurance", "insurance market", "property insurance"],
178	    "TEC": ["broadband", "telecommunications", "spectrum", "net neutrality", "5g",
179	            "rural broadband"],
180	    "COM": ["broadcasting", "local news", "television stations", "radio stations",
181	            "cable television"],
182	    "CPI": ["semiconductor", "semiconductors", "chips act", "data center",
183	            "data centers", "cloud computing"],
184	    "SCI": ["artificial intelligence", "ai regulation", "scientific research",
185	            "research and development", "national science foundation",
186	            "quantum computing", "stem education"],
187	    "TRD": ["trade agreement", "free trade", "trade deal", "trade war", "tariff",
188	            "tariffs", "exports", "imports", "world trade organization",
189	            "trade policy", "section 301", "trade deficit"],
190	    "LAW": ["law enforcement", "criminal justice", "violent crime", "police officers",
191	            "public safety", "sentencing reform", "mass incarceration"],
192	    "ALC": ["opioid", "opioids", "fentanyl", "drug abuse", "substance abuse",
193	            "overdose", "opioid crisis", "addiction"],
194	    "HOM": ["homeland security", "department of homeland security", "tsa",
195	            "cybersecurity", "cyberattack"],
196	    "FOR": ["foreign policy", "foreign relations", "foreign aid", "state department",
197	            "economic sanctions", "human rights abuses"],
198	    "AVI": ["airline", "airlines", "airport", "airports", "aviation", "faa",
199	            "air travel"],
200	    "RRR": ["railroad", "railroads", "freight rail", "passenger rail", "amtrak",
201	            "rail safety"],
202	    "TRA": ["public transit", "mass transit", "transportation infrastructure",
203	            "surface transportation"],
204	    "ROD": ["highway", "highways", "road construction"],
205	    "SMB": ["small business", "small businesses", "small business administration",
206	            "main street businesses"],
207	    "GAM": ["casino", "gambling", "sports betting", "online gambling"],
208	    "SPO": ["youth sports", "professional sports", "college athletics",
209	            "name image likeness"],
210	    "TOB": ["tobacco", "vaping", "e-cigarettes", "cigarettes"],
211	    "ANI": ["animal welfare", "animal cruelty", "endangered species",
212	            "wildlife protection"],
213	    "NAT": ["public lands", "national parks", "natural resources", "mining"],
214	}

216	
217	def _build_issue_matcher(mapping):
218	    """One alternation regex over all keywords + a lowercase keyword->code lookup.
219	
220	    Single pass per document (like BILL_RE). Longest keywords first so, e.g.,
221	    'health care costs' wins over 'health care' at the same position. Boundaries
222	    use \\w lookarounds (not \\b) so punctuated phrases like 'k-12' / '401(k)'
223	    and phrase edges behave.
224	    """
225	    kw_to_code = {}
226	    for code, kws in mapping.items():
227	        for kw in kws:
228	            canon = " ".join(kw.lower().split())
229	            if canon in kw_to_code and kw_to_code[canon] != code:
230	                raise ValueError(f"keyword {canon!r} maps to both "
231	                                 f"{kw_to_code[canon]} and {code}")
232	            kw_to_code[canon] = code
233	    alts = sorted(kw_to_code, key=len, reverse=True)
234	    body = "|".join(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in kw.split()) for kw in alts)
235	    pattern = re.compile(r"(?<![\w])(?:" + body + r")(?![\w])", re.I)
236	    return pattern, kw_to_code
237	
238	
239	ISSUE_RE, KEYWORD_TO_CODE = _build_issue_matcher(ISSUE_KEYWORDS)
240	
241	
242	def extract_issues(text):
243	    """Yield (issue_code, keyword) once per distinct keyword found in text."""
244	    if not text:
245	        return
246	    seen = set()
247	    for m in ISSUE_RE.finditer(text):
248	        canon = " ".join(m.group(0).lower().split())
249	        code = KEYWORD_TO_CODE.get(canon)
250	        if code and (code, canon) not in seen:
251	            seen.add((code, canon))
252	            yield code, canon
253	
254	
255	def get_any(d, *keys, default=None):
256	    """First non-empty value among candidate keys (Senate JSON field names vary)."""
257	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
258	        return default
259	    for k in keys:
260	        v = d.get(k)
261	        if v not in (None, "", [], {}):
262	            return v
263	    return default
264	
265	
266	def to_num(v):
267	    if v in (None, ""):
268	        return None
269	    try:
270	        return float(str(v).replace(",", ""))
271	    except ValueError:
272	        return None
273	
274	
275	def strip_or_none(v):
276	    if v is None:
277	        return None
278	    s = str(v).strip()
279	    return s or None

346	VIEWS = """
347	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW members AS
348	  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, any_value(party) AS party,
349	         any_value(state) AS state, any_value(chamber) AS chamber,
350	         count(*) AS n_releases
351	  FROM press_releases WHERE bioguide_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY bioguide_id;
352	
353	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_client_quarter AS
354	  SELECT client_name, filing_year, filing_period,
355	         sum(income) AS total_income, count(*) AS n_filings
356	  FROM senate_filings GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;
357	
358	-- Income is filing-level. Attributing it to each activity's issue code
359	-- overstates multi-issue filings. Use for ranking/trend, not for exact dollars.
360	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_issue_quarter AS
361	  SELECT a.general_issue_code, f.filing_year, f.filing_period,
362	         sum(f.income) AS attributed_income, count(DISTINCT f.filing_uuid) AS n_filings
363	  FROM senate_activities a JOIN senate_filings f USING (filing_uuid)
364	  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;
365	
366	-- Press-side analogue of v_spend_by_issue_quarter, on the SAME (filing_year,
367	-- filing_period) grain so the two join directly. n_releases = distinct releases
368	-- mentioning the code that quarter (the volume metric). n_keyword_hits is the
369	-- looser total. Press month -> Senate quarter label. See queries/press_issue_coupling.sql.
370	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_press_issue_quarter AS
371	  SELECT m.issue_code,
372	         CAST(substr(p.date, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) AS filing_year,
373	         CASE
374	           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 3 THEN 'first_quarter'
375	           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 4 AND 6 THEN 'second_quarter'
376	           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 7 AND 9 THEN 'third_quarter'
377	           ELSE 'fourth_quarter'
378	         END AS filing_period,
379	         count(DISTINCT m.pr_id) AS n_releases,
380	         count(*) AS n_keyword_hits
381	  FROM press_issue_mentions m JOIN press_releases p ON p.pr_id = m.pr_id
382	  WHERE p.date IS NOT NULL AND length(p.date) >= 7
383	  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;
384	
385	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_releases_by_member_month AS
386	  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, substr(date, 1, 7) AS month,
387	         count(*) AS n_releases
388	  FROM press_releases GROUP BY bioguide_id, substr(date, 1, 7);
389	
390	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_covered_positions AS
391	  SELECT 'senate' AS dataset, filing_uuid AS record_key, first_name, last_name,
392	         covered_position FROM senate_lobbyists
393	  WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL
394	  UNION ALL
395	  SELECT 'house', filing_id, first_name, last_name, covered_position
396	  FROM house_lobbyists WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL;
397	"""

399	
400	class Sink:
401	    """Batched inserts per table, staged through temp NDJSON files and COPY.
402	
403	    duckdb's executemany runs row-by-row (~100x slower). NDJSON staging is
404	    immune to the CSV-dialect ambiguity that multi-line press text triggers:
405	    json.dumps escapes every field onto one line.
406	    """
407	
408	    def __init__(self, con):
409	        self.con = con
410	        self.buf = {}
411	        self.counts = {}
412	        self.cols = {}
413	
414	    def _columns(self, table):
415	        if table not in self.cols:
416	            self.cols[table] = [r[1] for r in self.con.execute(
417	                f"PRAGMA table_info('{table}')").fetchall()]
418	        return self.cols[table]
419	
420	    def add(self, table, row):
421	        self.buf.setdefault(table, []).append(row)
422	        self.counts[table] = self.counts.get(table, 0) + 1
423	        if len(self.buf[table]) >= BATCH:
424	            self.flush(table)
425	
426	    def flush(self, table=None):
427	        import os
428	        import tempfile
429	        for t in [table] if table else list(self.buf):
430	            rows = self.buf.get(t) or []
431	            if not rows:
432	                continue
433	            cols = self._columns(t)
434	            fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".ndjson")
435	            os.close(fd)
436	            try:
437	                with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
438	                    for row in rows:
439	                        f.write(json.dumps(dict(zip(cols, row)),
440	                                           ensure_ascii=False, default=str))
441	                        f.write("\n")
442	                self.con.execute(
443	                    f"COPY {t} FROM '{tmp.replace(chr(92), '/')}' (FORMAT json)")
444	            finally:
445	                os.unlink(tmp)
446	            self.buf[t] = []
447	
448	
449	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- press
450	
451	def load_press(sink, data_root, years, months=None, max_records=None):
452	    root = data_root / "congress_press"
453	    files = sorted(root.glob("*.jsonl")) + sorted(root.glob("*/*.jsonl"))
454	    n = 0
455	    for path in files:
456	        m = re.match(r"(\d{4})-(\d{2})", path.stem)
457	        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
458	            continue
459	        if months and (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))) not in months:
460	            continue
461	        rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
462	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
463	            for line_no, line in enumerate(f, 1):
464	                line = line.strip()
465	                if not line:
466	                    continue
467	                rec = json.loads(line)
468	                mem = rec.get("member") or {}
469	                n += 1
470	                key = f"{rel}:{line_no}"
471	                sink.add("press_releases", (
472	                    n, rec.get("url"), rec.get("title"), rec.get("date"),
473	                    rec.get("date_source"), rec.get("source"), rec.get("domain"),
474	                    rec.get("scraper"), get_any(mem, "bioguide_id"),
475	                    get_any(mem, "name"), get_any(mem, "party"),
476	                    get_any(mem, "state"), get_any(mem, "chamber"),
477	                    rec.get("text"), rel, line_no))
478	                for bill, raw in extract_bills(rec.get("text") or ""):
479	                    sink.add("bill_mentions", ("press", key, bill, raw, rel))
480	                for code, kw in extract_issues(rec.get("text") or ""):
481	                    sink.add("press_issue_mentions", (n, code, kw, rel, line_no))
482	                if max_records and n >= max_records:
483	                    sink.flush()
484	                    return n
485	    sink.flush()
486	    return n
487

491	def load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records=None):
492	    done = {"filings": 0, "contributions": 0}
493	    for year in sorted(years):
494	        for kind in ("filings", "contributions"):
495	            if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
496	                continue
497	            path = data_root / "senate" / str(year) / kind / f"{kind}_{year}.json"
498	            if not path.exists():
499	                continue
500	            rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
501	            with open(path, "rb") as f:
502	                for idx, rec in enumerate(ijson.items(f, "item")):
503	                    if kind == "filings":
504	                        _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx)
505	                    else:
506	                        _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx)
507	                    done[kind] += 1
508	                    if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
509	                        break
510	    sink.flush()
511	    return done["filings"]

514	def _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx):
515	    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
516	    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
517	    cli = rec.get("client") or {}
518	    sink.add("senate_filings", (
519	        uuid, get_any(rec, "filing_type"), get_any(rec, "filing_period"),
520	        rec.get("filing_year"), to_num(get_any(rec, "income")),
521	        to_num(get_any(rec, "expenses")), strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "id")),
522	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")),
523	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "house_registrant_id")),
524	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "id", "client_id")),
525	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "name")), strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "state")),
526	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "general_description")),
527	        get_any(rec, "dt_posted", "posted"), rel, idx))
528	    for ai, act in enumerate(rec.get("lobbying_activities") or []):
529	        desc = strip_or_none(get_any(act, "description"))
530	        sink.add("senate_activities", (
531	            uuid, ai, get_any(act, "general_issue_code"), desc, rel, idx))
532	        for bill, raw in extract_bills(desc or ""):
533	            sink.add("bill_mentions", ("senate", uuid, bill, raw, rel))
534	        for lob in act.get("lobbyists") or []:
535	            person = lob.get("lobbyist") or lob
536	            sink.add("senate_lobbyists", (
537	                uuid, ai, strip_or_none(get_any(person, "first_name")),
538	                strip_or_none(get_any(person, "last_name")),
539	                strip_or_none(get_any(lob, "covered_position")),
540	                bool(get_any(lob, "new", "is_new", default=False)), rel, idx))
541	        for ge in act.get("government_entities") or []:
542	            name = ge.get("name") if isinstance(ge, dict) else ge
543	            sink.add("senate_gov_entities", (uuid, ai, strip_or_none(name), rel, idx))
544	    for fe in rec.get("foreign_entities") or []:
545	        if isinstance(fe, dict):
546	            sink.add("senate_foreign_entities", (
547	                uuid, strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "name")),
548	                strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "country")), rel, idx))
549	
550	
551	def _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx):
552	    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
553	    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
554	    lob = rec.get("lobbyist") or {}
555	    lob_name = " ".join(x for x in (get_any(lob, "first_name"),
556	                                    get_any(lob, "last_name")) if x) or None
557	    pacs = rec.get("pacs") or []
558	    pacs_txt = "; ".join(p if isinstance(p, str) else str(get_any(p, "name", default=p))
559	                         for p in pacs) or None
560	    sink.add("senate_contributions", (
561	        uuid, get_any(rec, "filer_type"), rec.get("filing_year"),
562	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")), lob_name, pacs_txt, rel, idx))
563	    for ii, item in enumerate(rec.get("contribution_items") or []):
564	        sink.add("senate_contribution_items", (
565	            uuid, ii, get_any(item, "contribution_type", "type"),
566	            to_num(get_any(item, "amount")),
567	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "payee_name", "payee")),
568	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "honoree_name", "honoree")),
569	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "contributor_name", "contributor")),
570	            get_any(item, "date", "contribution_date"), rel, idx))

574	
575	DIR_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d{4})_(Registrations|1stQuarter|2ndQuarter|3rdQuarter|4thQuarter)_XML$")
576	PERIOD = {"Registrations": "RR", "1stQuarter": "Q1", "2ndQuarter": "Q2",
577	          "3rdQuarter": "Q3", "4thQuarter": "Q4"}
578	
579	
580	def _strip_ns(root):
581	    for el in root.iter():
582	        if "}" in el.tag:
583	            el.tag = el.tag.split("}", 1)[1]
584	
585	
586	def _ft(root, *names):
587	    """First non-blank text among candidate tag names, searched anywhere."""
588	    for name in names:
589	        for el in root.iter(name):
590	            if el.text and el.text.strip():
591	                return el.text.strip()
592	    return None
593	
594	
595	def load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods=None, max_records=None):
596	    house = data_root / "house"
597	    n, errors = 0, []
598	    if not house.exists():
599	        return n, errors
600	    for d in sorted(house.iterdir()):
601	        m = DIR_RE.match(d.name) if d.is_dir() else None
602	        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
603	            continue
604	        year, period = int(m.group(1)), PERIOD[m.group(2)]
605	        if periods and period not in periods:
606	            continue
607	        for xml_path in sorted(d.glob("*.xml")):
608	            try:
609	                tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
610	            except ET.ParseError as e:
611	                errors.append(f"{xml_path.name}: {e}")
612	                continue
613	            root = tree.getroot()
614	            _strip_ns(root)
615	            fid = xml_path.stem
616	            rel = xml_path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
617	            form = "LD1" if root.tag.endswith("1") else "LD2"
618	
619	            def add_lobbyists(parent, ali_index):
620	                for lob in parent.iter("lobbyist"):
621	                    first = _ft(lob, "lobbyistFirstName", "firstName", "first_name")
622	                    last = _ft(lob, "lobbyistLastName", "lastName", "last_name")
623	                    if not (first or last):
624	                        continue  # forms pad with empty <lobbyist> slots
625	                    sink.add("house_lobbyists", (
626	                        fid, ali_index, first, last,
627	                        _ft(lob, "coveredPosition", "covered_position"),
628	                        _ft(lob, "lobbyistNew", "new"), rel))
629	
630	            all_desc = []
631	            ali_infos = root.findall(".//alis/ali_info")
632	            if ali_infos:
633	                # Quarterly (LD2) schema, verified against real 2026-Q1 files.
634	                for ai, ali in enumerate(ali_infos):
635	                    descs = [d.text.strip() for d in ali.iter("description")
636	                             if d.text and d.text.strip()]
637	                    desc = "\n".join(descs) or None
638	                    if desc:
639	                        all_desc.append(desc)
640	                    sink.add("house_alis", (
641	                        fid, ai, _ft(ali, "issueAreaCode", "ali_Code"), desc,
642	                        _ft(ali, "federal_agencies", "federalAgencies"), rel))
643	                    add_lobbyists(ali, ai)
644	            else:
645	                # Registration (LD1) / older layout per the data manual: flat
646	                # ali_Code list, lobbyists at document level. Verify when a
647	                # Registrations_XML directory lands.
648	                for ai, ali in enumerate(root.iter("ali_Code")):
649	                    if ali.text and ali.text.strip():
650	                        sink.add("house_alis", (fid, ai, ali.text.strip(),
651	                                                None, None, rel))
652	                descs = [d.text.strip() for d in root.findall(".//specific_issues//description")
653	                         if d.text and d.text.strip()]
654	                descs += [el.text.strip() for el in root.findall(".//specific_issues")
655	                          if el.text and el.text.strip()]
656	                all_desc.extend(descs)
657	                add_lobbyists(root, None)
658	
659	            spec = "\n".join(all_desc) or None
660	            sink.add("house_filings", (
661	                fid, form, _ft(root, "organizationName", "organizationname"),
662	                _ft(root, "clientName", "clientname"),
663	                _ft(root, "senateID", "senateId", "senateid"),
664	                _ft(root, "houseID", "houseId", "houseid"),
665	                year, period, to_num(_ft(root, "income")),
666	                to_num(_ft(root, "expenses")), spec, rel))
667	            for bill, raw in extract_bills(spec or ""):
668	                sink.add("bill_mentions", ("house", fid, bill, raw, rel))
669	            n += 1
670	            if max_records and n >= max_records:
671	                sink.flush()
672	                return n, errors
673	    sink.flush()
674	    return n, errors
675

677	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- sanity
678	
679	def sanity_report(con, db_path, years, house_errors):
680	    lines = ["# Sanity report", ""]
681	    tables = [r[0] for r in con.execute(
682	        "SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables "
683	        "WHERE table_type='BASE TABLE' ORDER BY 1").fetchall()]
684	    lines.append("| table | rows |")
685	    lines.append("|---|---|")
686	    counts = {}
687	    for t in tables:
688	        c = con.execute(f"SELECT count(*) FROM {t}").fetchone()[0]
689	        counts[t] = c
690	        lines.append(f"| {t} | {c:,} |")
691	    lines.append("")
692	    if 2025 in years:
693	        lines.append("## 2025 vs data manual")
694	        lines.append("")
695	        lines.append("| dataset | loaded (2025) | manual says | delta |")
696	        lines.append("|---|---|---|---|")
697	        loaded = {
698	            "press_releases": con.execute(
699	                "SELECT count(*) FROM press_releases WHERE src_file LIKE '%2025-%'"
700	            ).fetchone()[0],
701	            "senate_filings": con.execute(
702	                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_filings WHERE filing_year=2025"
703	            ).fetchone()[0],
704	            "senate_contributions": con.execute(
705	                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_contributions WHERE filing_year=2025"
706	            ).fetchone()[0],
707	            "house_filings": con.execute(
708	                "SELECT count(*) FROM house_filings WHERE report_year=2025"
709	            ).fetchone()[0],
710	        }
711	        for k, ref in MANUAL_2025.items():
712	            got = loaded[k]
713	            delta = (got - ref) / ref * 100 if ref else 0
714	            flag = "" if abs(delta) < 10 else "  ⚠️"
715	            lines.append(f"| {k} | {got:,} | ~{ref:,} | {delta:+.1f}%{flag} |")
716	        lines.append("")
717	        lines.append("Deltas beyond ±10% mean either a partial download or a parser "
718	                     "mismatch — investigate before trusting downstream queries.")
719	    if house_errors:
720	        lines.append("")
721	        lines.append(f"## House XML parse errors: {len(house_errors)}")
722	        lines.extend(f"- {e}" for e in house_errors[:20])
723	    report = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
724	    out = db_path.parent / "sanity_report.md"
725	    out.write_text(report, encoding="utf-8")
726	    print(report)
727	    print(f"Sanity report written to {out}")
728	
729	
730	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- main
731	
732	def main():
733	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
734	                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
735	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True, type=Path)
736	    ap.add_argument("--db", type=Path, default=Path("db/lda.duckdb"))
737	    ap.add_argument("--years", nargs="*", type=int,
738	                    help="Years to load (default: everything present)")
739	    ap.add_argument("--sample", metavar="YYYY-QN",
740	                    help="Smoke mode: one quarter of press+house, capped Senate records")
741	    ap.add_argument("--max-records", type=int,
742	                    help="Cap per-dataset primary records (smoke/testing)")
743	    args = ap.parse_args()
744	
745	    data_root = args.data_root
746	    if not data_root.exists():
747	        sys.exit(f"data root not found: {data_root}")
748	
749	    years = set(args.years or range(2022, 2027))
750	    months = periods = None
751	    max_records = args.max_records
752	    if args.sample:
753	        m = re.match(r"^(\d{4})-Q([1-4])$", args.sample)
754	        if not m:
755	            sys.exit("--sample must look like 2025-Q1")
756	        y, q = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
757	        years = {y}
758	        months = {(y, mm) for mm in range(3 * q - 2, 3 * q + 1)}
759	        periods = {"RR", f"Q{q}"}
760	        max_records = max_records or 5000
761	
762	    args.db.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
763	    con = duckdb.connect(str(args.db))
764	    for stmt in DDL.split(";"):
765	        if stmt.strip():
766	            con.execute(stmt)
767	
768	    import time as _time
769	    sink = Sink(con)
770	    t0 = _time.time()
771	    print(f"Loading press releases ({sorted(years)}) ...")
772	    n_press = load_press(sink, data_root, years, months, max_records)
773	    t1 = _time.time()
774	    print(f"  {n_press:,} releases in {t1 - t0:.1f}s")
775	    print("Loading Senate LDA ...")
776	    n_sen = load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records)
777	    t2 = _time.time()
778	    print(f"  {n_sen:,} filings in {t2 - t1:.1f}s")
779	    print("Loading House LDA ...")
780	    n_house, house_errors = load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods, max_records)
781	    t3 = _time.time()
782	    print(f"  {n_house:,} filings ({len(house_errors)} parse errors) in {t3 - t2:.1f}s")
783	
784	    for stmt in VIEWS.split(";"):
785	        if stmt.strip():
786	            con.execute(stmt)
787	
788	    sanity_report(con, args.db, years, house_errors)
789	    con.close()
790	    print(f"Done: {args.db}")
791	
792	
793	if __name__ == "__main__":
794	    main()
795

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build the GAIN investigation database from the raw challenge corpus.

Parses three datasets (layout per the challenge data manual) into one DuckDB file:

  <data-root>/congress_press/   JSONL press releases (year dirs + 2026 files at root)
  <data-root>/senate/           Senate LDA filings + contributions (JSON arrays, streamed)
  <data-root>/house/            House LDA registrations + quarterlies (one XML per filing)

Guarantees:
  * Every row carries a raw-record pointer (src_file + src_line/src_index, or the XML
    path) so any query result resolves to a citable raw record in one step
    (see show_record.py).
  * A sanity report reconciles row counts against the data manual's published 2025
    scale before the database is trusted. Written next to the DB as sanity_report.md.

Usage:
  python build_db.py --data-root data/                       # everything present
  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --years 2025 2026     # pilot slice
  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --sample 2025-Q1      # smoke mode (minutes)

Caveat: Senate JSON key names and House XML tag names were drafted from the data
manual before the raw data landed and are coded defensively (multiple candidate
keys/tags, strip everything). The sanity report is the tripwire for mismatches —
verify against real data before trusting downstream queries.
"""

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

import duckdb
import ijson

BATCH = 50000

# Sanity report contains non-ASCII; Windows pipes default to cp1252.
if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")

# Data manual's published 2025 scale, used by the sanity report.
MANUAL_2025 = {
    "press_releases": 48000,        # "~48K releases in 2025"
    "senate_filings": 108225,
    "senate_contributions": 39438,
    "house_filings": 108522,        # ~7,762 registrations + ~100,760 quarterlies
}

# Bill citations: longest alternatives first so "H.J.Res." doesn't match as "H.R.".
BILL_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b("
    r"H\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*R\.?|S\.?"
    r")\s*(\d{1,5})\b",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)


def norm_bill(prefix: str, number: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r"[^A-Z]", "", prefix.upper()) + number


def extract_bills(text):
    if not text:
        return
    for m in BILL_RE.finditer(text):
        yield norm_bill(m.group(1), m.group(2)), m.group(0)


# ---------------------------------------------------------- press issue tagging
#
# Tags press-release text with ALI issue codes (data/senate/constants/
# lobbying_activity_issues.json) using a curated keyword vocabulary, so press
# volume on an issue can be compared to lobbying spend on the same code
# (v_spend_by_issue_quarter). This is the press-side analogue of extract_bills:
# one raw-record-pointer'd row per (issue_code, keyword) that appears in a release.
#
# DESIGN / PROVENANCE OF THE MAPPING (read before trusting any downstream count):
#   * Precision over recall. Keywords are distinctive phrases, not bare topic words
#     ("border security" not "border"; "national defense" not "defense"), matched
#     on whole-word boundaries, case-insensitively, with flexible interior
#     whitespace (so "health\ncare" across a line break still matches "health care").
#   * Each keyword string maps to exactly ONE code (asserted at build time), so a
#     hit is unambiguously attributable. Where a concept could belong to two codes,
#     it is assigned to the closest ALI code and flagged in SHAKY_MAPPINGS below.
#   * NOT exhaustive over the ~79 ALI codes. It covers the codes congressional
#     press releases actually discuss, with vocabulary specific enough to trust.
#   * A "mention" is deduped per release: one row per (pr_id, issue_code, keyword),
#     never one-per-occurrence, so a release repeating "health care" ten times does
#     not outweigh ten releases mentioning it once. Volume metrics downstream should
#     COUNT(DISTINCT pr_id).
#
# Known ambiguities are recorded here (not hidden) and in the coupling write-up:
SHAKY_MAPPINGS = """
  tariff/tariffs -> TRD (Trade), NOT TAR: ALI's TAR code is narrowly "miscellaneous
    tariff bills"; LDA filers file broad tariff/trade-war policy under TRD, so press
    "tariffs" is mapped to TRD to keep the say-vs-pay join meaningful. Watch this one.
  health cluster HCR/MED/MMM/PHA/ALC bleeds: ACA "tax credits" tag BOTH HCR and TAX;
    "prescription drug" -> PHA but reads as HCR; medicare/medicaid -> MMM; opioid/
    fentanyl -> ALC though often framed as LAW or HCR.
  tech cluster CPI/SCI/TEC: "artificial intelligence" -> SCI, "semiconductor" -> CPI,
    "broadband/spectrum" -> TEC. Real releases blur these; treat the three as one
    loose "tech" bucket when reading trends.
  social security -> RET (no dedicated SS code exists; RET=Retirement is closest;
    it is arguably WEL). federal reserve -> BAN (could be MON). background check ->
    FIR (assumes firearms context; could be employment). insurance premiums are NOT
    mapped to INS (they are overwhelmingly health-context) -> INS is flood/auto/
    property only and is a weak, low-volume code. COM/SPO/GAM/ANI are low-precision
    breadth codes, included but noisy.
"""

# code -> list of distinctive keyword phrases (lowercase, single-spaced).
ISSUE_KEYWORDS = {
    "HCR": ["health care", "healthcare", "affordable care act", "obamacare",
            "health insurance", "health coverage", "health care costs",
            "health care system", "public health", "health care coverage"],
    "MMM": ["medicare", "medicaid", "medicare advantage", "dual eligible"],
    "PHA": ["prescription drug", "prescription drugs", "drug prices", "drug pricing",
            "pharmacy", "pharmacist", "pharmacy benefit", "insulin"],
    "MED": ["medical research", "clinical trial", "clinical trials",
            "disease research", "national institutes of health", "biomedical research"],
    "TAX": ["tax credit", "tax credits", "tax cut", "tax cuts", "tax reform",
            "tax code", "taxation", "internal revenue service", "income tax",
            "corporate tax", "child tax credit", "estate tax"],
    "BUD": ["appropriations", "government shutdown", "debt ceiling", "federal budget",
            "budget deficit", "continuing resolution", "national debt", "debt limit",
            "discretionary spending"],
    "IMM": ["immigration", "immigrant", "immigrants", "border security",
            "southern border", "undocumented", "asylum seekers", "daca",
            "deportation", "border crossing", "illegal immigration"],
    "DEF": ["national defense", "defense department", "department of defense",
            "pentagon", "ndaa", "national defense authorization", "armed forces",
            "military readiness", "defense spending", "servicemembers"],
    "FIR": ["gun violence", "gun control", "gun safety", "firearm", "firearms",
            "second amendment", "assault weapons", "background check",
            "background checks", "ghost guns"],
    "ENG": ["clean energy", "renewable energy", "nuclear power", "energy policy",
            "energy prices", "power grid", "electric grid", "solar energy",
            "wind energy", "energy independence", "energy costs"],
    "ENV": ["climate change", "environmental protection", "greenhouse gas",
            "greenhouse gases", "carbon emissions", "superfund", "air pollution",
            "climate crisis", "environmental protection agency"],
    "CAW": ["clean water", "clean air", "drinking water", "air quality",
            "water quality", "pfas", "safe drinking water"],
    "FUE": ["gas prices", "gasoline prices", "oil and gas", "crude oil",
            "offshore drilling", "natural gas", "fossil fuels"],
    "AGR": ["farm bill", "farmers", "agriculture", "agricultural", "crop insurance",
            "department of agriculture", "livestock"],
    "VET": ["veterans", "veteran", "veterans affairs", "department of veterans affairs",
            "gi bill", "va health care", "veterans benefits"],
    "EDU": ["student loan", "student loans", "student debt", "public schools",
            "higher education", "department of education", "pell grant",
            "pell grants", "k-12", "school funding", "college affordability", "teachers"],
    "LBR": ["labor union", "labor unions", "minimum wage", "workers rights",
            "collective bargaining", "overtime pay", "workplace safety",
            "right to work", "project labor agreement", "antitrust", "unionize"],
    "HOU": ["affordable housing", "homelessness", "public housing", "housing costs",
            "housing affordability", "homeless", "section 8"],
    "RET": ["social security", "retirement savings", "pension", "pensions", "401(k)",
            "retirement security"],
    "CIV": ["civil rights", "voting rights", "civil liberties", "voter suppression",
            "lgbtq", "discrimination"],
    "FAM": ["abortion", "reproductive rights", "pro-life", "pro-choice",
            "paid family leave", "planned parenthood"],
    "POS": ["postal service", "usps", "post office", "postal facilities",
            "letter carriers"],
    "FIN": ["wall street", "securities and exchange", "cryptocurrency", "crypto",
            "stablecoin", "digital assets", "private equity", "hedge fund",
            "stock market", "sec regulation"],
    "BAN": ["banking", "banks", "community banks", "credit union", "credit unions",
            "federal reserve", "bank regulation"],
    "INS": ["flood insurance", "auto insurance", "insurance market", "property insurance"],
    "TEC": ["broadband", "telecommunications", "spectrum", "net neutrality", "5g",
            "rural broadband"],
    "COM": ["broadcasting", "local news", "television stations", "radio stations",
            "cable television"],
    "CPI": ["semiconductor", "semiconductors", "chips act", "data center",
            "data centers", "cloud computing"],
    "SCI": ["artificial intelligence", "ai regulation", "scientific research",
            "research and development", "national science foundation",
            "quantum computing", "stem education"],
    "TRD": ["trade agreement", "free trade", "trade deal", "trade war", "tariff",
            "tariffs", "exports", "imports", "world trade organization",
            "trade policy", "section 301", "trade deficit"],
    "LAW": ["law enforcement", "criminal justice", "violent crime", "police officers",
            "public safety", "sentencing reform", "mass incarceration"],
    "ALC": ["opioid", "opioids", "fentanyl", "drug abuse", "substance abuse",
            "overdose", "opioid crisis", "addiction"],
    "HOM": ["homeland security", "department of homeland security", "tsa",
            "cybersecurity", "cyberattack"],
    "FOR": ["foreign policy", "foreign relations", "foreign aid", "state department",
            "economic sanctions", "human rights abuses"],
    "AVI": ["airline", "airlines", "airport", "airports", "aviation", "faa",
            "air travel"],
    "RRR": ["railroad", "railroads", "freight rail", "passenger rail", "amtrak",
            "rail safety"],
    "TRA": ["public transit", "mass transit", "transportation infrastructure",
            "surface transportation"],
    "ROD": ["highway", "highways", "road construction"],
    "SMB": ["small business", "small businesses", "small business administration",
            "main street businesses"],
    "GAM": ["casino", "gambling", "sports betting", "online gambling"],
    "SPO": ["youth sports", "professional sports", "college athletics",
            "name image likeness"],
    "TOB": ["tobacco", "vaping", "e-cigarettes", "cigarettes"],
    "ANI": ["animal welfare", "animal cruelty", "endangered species",
            "wildlife protection"],
    "NAT": ["public lands", "national parks", "natural resources", "mining"],
}


def _build_issue_matcher(mapping):
    """One alternation regex over all keywords + a lowercase keyword->code lookup.

    Single pass per document (like BILL_RE). Longest keywords first so, e.g.,
    'health care costs' wins over 'health care' at the same position. Boundaries
    use \\w lookarounds (not \\b) so punctuated phrases like 'k-12' / '401(k)'
    and phrase edges behave.
    """
    kw_to_code = {}
    for code, kws in mapping.items():
        for kw in kws:
            canon = " ".join(kw.lower().split())
            if canon in kw_to_code and kw_to_code[canon] != code:
                raise ValueError(f"keyword {canon!r} maps to both "
                                 f"{kw_to_code[canon]} and {code}")
            kw_to_code[canon] = code
    alts = sorted(kw_to_code, key=len, reverse=True)
    body = "|".join(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in kw.split()) for kw in alts)
    pattern = re.compile(r"(?<![\w])(?:" + body + r")(?![\w])", re.I)
    return pattern, kw_to_code


ISSUE_RE, KEYWORD_TO_CODE = _build_issue_matcher(ISSUE_KEYWORDS)


def extract_issues(text):
    """Yield (issue_code, keyword) once per distinct keyword found in text."""
    if not text:
        return
    seen = set()
    for m in ISSUE_RE.finditer(text):
        canon = " ".join(m.group(0).lower().split())
        code = KEYWORD_TO_CODE.get(canon)
        if code and (code, canon) not in seen:
            seen.add((code, canon))
            yield code, canon


def get_any(d, *keys, default=None):
    """First non-empty value among candidate keys (Senate JSON field names vary)."""
    if not isinstance(d, dict):
        return default
    for k in keys:
        v = d.get(k)
        if v not in (None, "", [], {}):
            return v
    return default


def to_num(v):
    if v in (None, ""):
        return None
    try:
        return float(str(v).replace(",", ""))
    except ValueError:
        return None


def strip_or_none(v):
    if v is None:
        return None
    s = str(v).strip()
    return s or None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- schema

DDL = """
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS press_releases (
  pr_id BIGINT, url TEXT, title TEXT, date TEXT, date_source TEXT, source TEXT,
  domain TEXT, scraper TEXT, bioguide_id TEXT, member_name TEXT, party TEXT,
  state TEXT, chamber TEXT, text TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_line INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_filings (
  filing_uuid TEXT, filing_type TEXT, filing_period TEXT, filing_year INTEGER,
  income DOUBLE, expenses DOUBLE, registrant_id TEXT, registrant_name TEXT,
  house_registrant_id TEXT, client_id TEXT, client_name TEXT, client_state TEXT,
  client_description TEXT, posted TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_activities (
  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, general_issue_code TEXT,
  description TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_lobbyists (
  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, first_name TEXT, last_name TEXT,
  covered_position TEXT, is_new BOOLEAN, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_gov_entities (
  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, entity_name TEXT,
  src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_foreign_entities (
  filing_uuid TEXT, name TEXT, country TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_contributions (
  filing_uuid TEXT, filer_type TEXT, filing_year INTEGER, registrant_name TEXT,
  lobbyist_name TEXT, pacs TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_contribution_items (
  filing_uuid TEXT, item_index INTEGER, contribution_type TEXT, amount DOUBLE,
  payee TEXT, honoree TEXT, contributor_name TEXT, date TEXT,
  src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_filings (
  filing_id TEXT, form TEXT, organization_name TEXT, client_name TEXT,
  senate_reg_id TEXT, house_reg_id TEXT, report_year INTEGER, report_period TEXT,
  income DOUBLE, expenses DOUBLE, specific_issues TEXT, src_path TEXT);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_lobbyists (
  filing_id TEXT, ali_index INTEGER, first_name TEXT, last_name TEXT,
  covered_position TEXT, lobbyist_new TEXT, src_path TEXT);

-- One row per <alis><ali_info> block (real quarterly schema, verified 2026-07-04).
-- federal_agencies is the raw comma-separated string. Splitting it into clean
-- entities is entity-resolver work, not loader work.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_alis (
  filing_id TEXT, ali_index INTEGER, issue_code TEXT, specific_issues TEXT,
  federal_agencies TEXT, src_path TEXT);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bill_mentions (
  dataset TEXT, record_key TEXT, bill TEXT, raw_match TEXT, src TEXT);

-- Press releases tagged with ALI issue codes via ISSUE_KEYWORDS. One row per
-- release/issue_code/keyword. pr_id + src_file:src_line is the raw-record
-- pointer (resolvable with show_record.py). See extract_issues / SHAKY_MAPPINGS.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS press_issue_mentions (
  pr_id BIGINT, issue_code TEXT, keyword TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_line INTEGER);
"""

VIEWS = """
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW members AS
  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, any_value(party) AS party,
         any_value(state) AS state, any_value(chamber) AS chamber,
         count(*) AS n_releases
  FROM press_releases WHERE bioguide_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY bioguide_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_client_quarter AS
  SELECT client_name, filing_year, filing_period,
         sum(income) AS total_income, count(*) AS n_filings
  FROM senate_filings GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;

-- Income is filing-level. Attributing it to each activity's issue code
-- overstates multi-issue filings. Use for ranking/trend, not for exact dollars.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_issue_quarter AS
  SELECT a.general_issue_code, f.filing_year, f.filing_period,
         sum(f.income) AS attributed_income, count(DISTINCT f.filing_uuid) AS n_filings
  FROM senate_activities a JOIN senate_filings f USING (filing_uuid)
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;

-- Press-side analogue of v_spend_by_issue_quarter, on the SAME (filing_year,
-- filing_period) grain so the two join directly. n_releases = distinct releases
-- mentioning the code that quarter (the volume metric). n_keyword_hits is the
-- looser total. Press month -> Senate quarter label. See queries/press_issue_coupling.sql.
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_press_issue_quarter AS
  SELECT m.issue_code,
         CAST(substr(p.date, 1, 4) AS INTEGER) AS filing_year,
         CASE
           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 3 THEN 'first_quarter'
           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 4 AND 6 THEN 'second_quarter'
           WHEN CAST(substr(p.date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 7 AND 9 THEN 'third_quarter'
           ELSE 'fourth_quarter'
         END AS filing_period,
         count(DISTINCT m.pr_id) AS n_releases,
         count(*) AS n_keyword_hits
  FROM press_issue_mentions m JOIN press_releases p ON p.pr_id = m.pr_id
  WHERE p.date IS NOT NULL AND length(p.date) >= 7
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_releases_by_member_month AS
  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, substr(date, 1, 7) AS month,
         count(*) AS n_releases
  FROM press_releases GROUP BY bioguide_id, substr(date, 1, 7);

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_covered_positions AS
  SELECT 'senate' AS dataset, filing_uuid AS record_key, first_name, last_name,
         covered_position FROM senate_lobbyists
  WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'house', filing_id, first_name, last_name, covered_position
  FROM house_lobbyists WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL;
"""


class Sink:
    """Batched inserts per table, staged through temp NDJSON files and COPY.

    duckdb's executemany runs row-by-row (~100x slower). NDJSON staging is
    immune to the CSV-dialect ambiguity that multi-line press text triggers:
    json.dumps escapes every field onto one line.
    """

    def __init__(self, con):
        self.con = con
        self.buf = {}
        self.counts = {}
        self.cols = {}

    def _columns(self, table):
        if table not in self.cols:
            self.cols[table] = [r[1] for r in self.con.execute(
                f"PRAGMA table_info('{table}')").fetchall()]
        return self.cols[table]

    def add(self, table, row):
        self.buf.setdefault(table, []).append(row)
        self.counts[table] = self.counts.get(table, 0) + 1
        if len(self.buf[table]) >= BATCH:
            self.flush(table)

    def flush(self, table=None):
        import os
        import tempfile
        for t in [table] if table else list(self.buf):
            rows = self.buf.get(t) or []
            if not rows:
                continue
            cols = self._columns(t)
            fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".ndjson")
            os.close(fd)
            try:
                with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                    for row in rows:
                        f.write(json.dumps(dict(zip(cols, row)),
                                           ensure_ascii=False, default=str))
                        f.write("\n")
                self.con.execute(
                    f"COPY {t} FROM '{tmp.replace(chr(92), '/')}' (FORMAT json)")
            finally:
                os.unlink(tmp)
            self.buf[t] = []


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- press

def load_press(sink, data_root, years, months=None, max_records=None):
    root = data_root / "congress_press"
    files = sorted(root.glob("*.jsonl")) + sorted(root.glob("*/*.jsonl"))
    n = 0
    for path in files:
        m = re.match(r"(\d{4})-(\d{2})", path.stem)
        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
            continue
        if months and (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))) not in months:
            continue
        rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
            for line_no, line in enumerate(f, 1):
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                rec = json.loads(line)
                mem = rec.get("member") or {}
                n += 1
                key = f"{rel}:{line_no}"
                sink.add("press_releases", (
                    n, rec.get("url"), rec.get("title"), rec.get("date"),
                    rec.get("date_source"), rec.get("source"), rec.get("domain"),
                    rec.get("scraper"), get_any(mem, "bioguide_id"),
                    get_any(mem, "name"), get_any(mem, "party"),
                    get_any(mem, "state"), get_any(mem, "chamber"),
                    rec.get("text"), rel, line_no))
                for bill, raw in extract_bills(rec.get("text") or ""):
                    sink.add("bill_mentions", ("press", key, bill, raw, rel))
                for code, kw in extract_issues(rec.get("text") or ""):
                    sink.add("press_issue_mentions", (n, code, kw, rel, line_no))
                if max_records and n >= max_records:
                    sink.flush()
                    return n
    sink.flush()
    return n


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- senate

def load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records=None):
    done = {"filings": 0, "contributions": 0}
    for year in sorted(years):
        for kind in ("filings", "contributions"):
            if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
                continue
            path = data_root / "senate" / str(year) / kind / f"{kind}_{year}.json"
            if not path.exists():
                continue
            rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
            with open(path, "rb") as f:
                for idx, rec in enumerate(ijson.items(f, "item")):
                    if kind == "filings":
                        _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx)
                    else:
                        _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx)
                    done[kind] += 1
                    if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
                        break
    sink.flush()
    return done["filings"]


def _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx):
    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
    cli = rec.get("client") or {}
    sink.add("senate_filings", (
        uuid, get_any(rec, "filing_type"), get_any(rec, "filing_period"),
        rec.get("filing_year"), to_num(get_any(rec, "income")),
        to_num(get_any(rec, "expenses")), strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "id")),
        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")),
        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "house_registrant_id")),
        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "id", "client_id")),
        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "name")), strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "state")),
        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "general_description")),
        get_any(rec, "dt_posted", "posted"), rel, idx))
    for ai, act in enumerate(rec.get("lobbying_activities") or []):
        desc = strip_or_none(get_any(act, "description"))
        sink.add("senate_activities", (
            uuid, ai, get_any(act, "general_issue_code"), desc, rel, idx))
        for bill, raw in extract_bills(desc or ""):
            sink.add("bill_mentions", ("senate", uuid, bill, raw, rel))
        for lob in act.get("lobbyists") or []:
            person = lob.get("lobbyist") or lob
            sink.add("senate_lobbyists", (
                uuid, ai, strip_or_none(get_any(person, "first_name")),
                strip_or_none(get_any(person, "last_name")),
                strip_or_none(get_any(lob, "covered_position")),
                bool(get_any(lob, "new", "is_new", default=False)), rel, idx))
        for ge in act.get("government_entities") or []:
            name = ge.get("name") if isinstance(ge, dict) else ge
            sink.add("senate_gov_entities", (uuid, ai, strip_or_none(name), rel, idx))
    for fe in rec.get("foreign_entities") or []:
        if isinstance(fe, dict):
            sink.add("senate_foreign_entities", (
                uuid, strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "name")),
                strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "country")), rel, idx))


def _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx):
    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
    lob = rec.get("lobbyist") or {}
    lob_name = " ".join(x for x in (get_any(lob, "first_name"),
                                    get_any(lob, "last_name")) if x) or None
    pacs = rec.get("pacs") or []
    pacs_txt = "; ".join(p if isinstance(p, str) else str(get_any(p, "name", default=p))
                         for p in pacs) or None
    sink.add("senate_contributions", (
        uuid, get_any(rec, "filer_type"), rec.get("filing_year"),
        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")), lob_name, pacs_txt, rel, idx))
    for ii, item in enumerate(rec.get("contribution_items") or []):
        sink.add("senate_contribution_items", (
            uuid, ii, get_any(item, "contribution_type", "type"),
            to_num(get_any(item, "amount")),
            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "payee_name", "payee")),
            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "honoree_name", "honoree")),
            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "contributor_name", "contributor")),
            get_any(item, "date", "contribution_date"), rel, idx))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- house

DIR_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d{4})_(Registrations|1stQuarter|2ndQuarter|3rdQuarter|4thQuarter)_XML$")
PERIOD = {"Registrations": "RR", "1stQuarter": "Q1", "2ndQuarter": "Q2",
          "3rdQuarter": "Q3", "4thQuarter": "Q4"}


def _strip_ns(root):
    for el in root.iter():
        if "}" in el.tag:
            el.tag = el.tag.split("}", 1)[1]


def _ft(root, *names):
    """First non-blank text among candidate tag names, searched anywhere."""
    for name in names:
        for el in root.iter(name):
            if el.text and el.text.strip():
                return el.text.strip()
    return None


def load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods=None, max_records=None):
    house = data_root / "house"
    n, errors = 0, []
    if not house.exists():
        return n, errors
    for d in sorted(house.iterdir()):
        m = DIR_RE.match(d.name) if d.is_dir() else None
        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
            continue
        year, period = int(m.group(1)), PERIOD[m.group(2)]
        if periods and period not in periods:
            continue
        for xml_path in sorted(d.glob("*.xml")):
            try:
                tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
            except ET.ParseError as e:
                errors.append(f"{xml_path.name}: {e}")
                continue
            root = tree.getroot()
            _strip_ns(root)
            fid = xml_path.stem
            rel = xml_path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
            form = "LD1" if root.tag.endswith("1") else "LD2"

            def add_lobbyists(parent, ali_index):
                for lob in parent.iter("lobbyist"):
                    first = _ft(lob, "lobbyistFirstName", "firstName", "first_name")
                    last = _ft(lob, "lobbyistLastName", "lastName", "last_name")
                    if not (first or last):
                        continue  # forms pad with empty <lobbyist> slots
                    sink.add("house_lobbyists", (
                        fid, ali_index, first, last,
                        _ft(lob, "coveredPosition", "covered_position"),
                        _ft(lob, "lobbyistNew", "new"), rel))

            all_desc = []
            ali_infos = root.findall(".//alis/ali_info")
            if ali_infos:
                # Quarterly (LD2) schema, verified against real 2026-Q1 files.
                for ai, ali in enumerate(ali_infos):
                    descs = [d.text.strip() for d in ali.iter("description")
                             if d.text and d.text.strip()]
                    desc = "\n".join(descs) or None
                    if desc:
                        all_desc.append(desc)
                    sink.add("house_alis", (
                        fid, ai, _ft(ali, "issueAreaCode", "ali_Code"), desc,
                        _ft(ali, "federal_agencies", "federalAgencies"), rel))
                    add_lobbyists(ali, ai)
            else:
                # Registration (LD1) / older layout per the data manual: flat
                # ali_Code list, lobbyists at document level. Verify when a
                # Registrations_XML directory lands.
                for ai, ali in enumerate(root.iter("ali_Code")):
                    if ali.text and ali.text.strip():
                        sink.add("house_alis", (fid, ai, ali.text.strip(),
                                                None, None, rel))
                descs = [d.text.strip() for d in root.findall(".//specific_issues//description")
                         if d.text and d.text.strip()]
                descs += [el.text.strip() for el in root.findall(".//specific_issues")
                          if el.text and el.text.strip()]
                all_desc.extend(descs)
                add_lobbyists(root, None)

            spec = "\n".join(all_desc) or None
            sink.add("house_filings", (
                fid, form, _ft(root, "organizationName", "organizationname"),
                _ft(root, "clientName", "clientname"),
                _ft(root, "senateID", "senateId", "senateid"),
                _ft(root, "houseID", "houseId", "houseid"),
                year, period, to_num(_ft(root, "income")),
                to_num(_ft(root, "expenses")), spec, rel))
            for bill, raw in extract_bills(spec or ""):
                sink.add("bill_mentions", ("house", fid, bill, raw, rel))
            n += 1
            if max_records and n >= max_records:
                sink.flush()
                return n, errors
    sink.flush()
    return n, errors


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- sanity

def sanity_report(con, db_path, years, house_errors):
    lines = ["# Sanity report", ""]
    tables = [r[0] for r in con.execute(
        "SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables "
        "WHERE table_type='BASE TABLE' ORDER BY 1").fetchall()]
    lines.append("| table | rows |")
    lines.append("|---|---|")
    counts = {}
    for t in tables:
        c = con.execute(f"SELECT count(*) FROM {t}").fetchone()[0]
        counts[t] = c
        lines.append(f"| {t} | {c:,} |")
    lines.append("")
    if 2025 in years:
        lines.append("## 2025 vs data manual")
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("| dataset | loaded (2025) | manual says | delta |")
        lines.append("|---|---|---|---|")
        loaded = {
            "press_releases": con.execute(
                "SELECT count(*) FROM press_releases WHERE src_file LIKE '%2025-%'"
            ).fetchone()[0],
            "senate_filings": con.execute(
                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_filings WHERE filing_year=2025"
            ).fetchone()[0],
            "senate_contributions": con.execute(
                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_contributions WHERE filing_year=2025"
            ).fetchone()[0],
            "house_filings": con.execute(
                "SELECT count(*) FROM house_filings WHERE report_year=2025"
            ).fetchone()[0],
        }
        for k, ref in MANUAL_2025.items():
            got = loaded[k]
            delta = (got - ref) / ref * 100 if ref else 0
            flag = "" if abs(delta) < 10 else "  ⚠️"
            lines.append(f"| {k} | {got:,} | ~{ref:,} | {delta:+.1f}%{flag} |")
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("Deltas beyond ±10% mean either a partial download or a parser "
                     "mismatch — investigate before trusting downstream queries.")
    if house_errors:
        lines.append("")
        lines.append(f"## House XML parse errors: {len(house_errors)}")
        lines.extend(f"- {e}" for e in house_errors[:20])
    report = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
    out = db_path.parent / "sanity_report.md"
    out.write_text(report, encoding="utf-8")
    print(report)
    print(f"Sanity report written to {out}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------- main

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True, type=Path)
    ap.add_argument("--db", type=Path, default=Path("db/lda.duckdb"))
    ap.add_argument("--years", nargs="*", type=int,
                    help="Years to load (default: everything present)")
    ap.add_argument("--sample", metavar="YYYY-QN",
                    help="Smoke mode: one quarter of press+house, capped Senate records")
    ap.add_argument("--max-records", type=int,
                    help="Cap per-dataset primary records (smoke/testing)")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    data_root = args.data_root
    if not data_root.exists():
        sys.exit(f"data root not found: {data_root}")

    years = set(args.years or range(2022, 2027))
    months = periods = None
    max_records = args.max_records
    if args.sample:
        m = re.match(r"^(\d{4})-Q([1-4])$", args.sample)
        if not m:
            sys.exit("--sample must look like 2025-Q1")
        y, q = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
        years = {y}
        months = {(y, mm) for mm in range(3 * q - 2, 3 * q + 1)}
        periods = {"RR", f"Q{q}"}
        max_records = max_records or 5000

    args.db.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    con = duckdb.connect(str(args.db))
    for stmt in DDL.split(";"):
        if stmt.strip():
            con.execute(stmt)

    import time as _time
    sink = Sink(con)
    t0 = _time.time()
    print(f"Loading press releases ({sorted(years)}) ...")
    n_press = load_press(sink, data_root, years, months, max_records)
    t1 = _time.time()
    print(f"  {n_press:,} releases in {t1 - t0:.1f}s")
    print("Loading Senate LDA ...")
    n_sen = load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records)
    t2 = _time.time()
    print(f"  {n_sen:,} filings in {t2 - t1:.1f}s")
    print("Loading House LDA ...")
    n_house, house_errors = load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods, max_records)
    t3 = _time.time()
    print(f"  {n_house:,} filings ({len(house_errors)} parse errors) in {t3 - t2:.1f}s")

    for stmt in VIEWS.split(";"):
        if stmt.strip():
            con.execute(stmt)

    sanity_report(con, args.db, years, house_errors)
    con.close()
    print(f"Done: {args.db}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
