content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Bill cross-check: given a bill (number OR named alias), list every Senate
3	filing, House filing, and press release that touches it, each with a citation
4	key resolvable by lda-corpus-loader/show_record.py.
5	
6	Why this exists (the L004 lesson). Press releases and members name bills ("the
7	Farm Bill", "NDAA", "Inflation Reduction Act"); LDA filings cite H.R./S. numbers.
8	Matching on numbers alone fabricates "lobbied but publicly silent" bills. This
9	tool bridges the two: a query by number also surfaces the name-cited press (and,
10	for phrase-primary bills, the name-cited filings), and a query by name reaches
11	the number-cited filings via the curated crosswalk in bill_aliases.json.
12	
13	  Number side  -> exact match on the pre-extracted `bill_mentions` table
14	  Name side    -> whole-word regex on press text (+ filing free-text on request)
15	
16	Usage:
17	  python bill_lookup.py <bill-or-alias> [options]
18	    <bill-or-alias>   "HR5376" | "H.R. 5376" | "Inflation Reduction Act" | "Farm Bill"
19	  --db PATH           DuckDB (default db/lda_full.duckdb)
20	  --aliases PATH      alias table (default: bill_aliases.json beside this script)
21	  --dataset SET       senate,house,press  (comma list; default all)
22	  --limit N           sample record keys shown per dataset/mode (default 8)
23	  --top N             top clients/members ranked (default 10)
24	  --scan-freetext     also NAME-match filing free-text (senate activity
25	                      descriptions, house specific_issues). Auto-on for
26	                      phrase-primary bills (no reliable number, e.g. Farm Bill).
27	  --data-root PATH    printed into the show_record.py hint (default ../../../data/data)
28	  --json              machine-readable output
29	  --list-aliases      print the alias table and exit
30	
31	Aggregate counts/rankings this prints are also expressible as a labeled block in
32	queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql (the citeable form for findings).
33	"""
34	
35	import argparse
36	import json
37	import re
38	import sys
39	from pathlib import Path
40	
41	import duckdb
42	
43	if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
44	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")
45	
46	# Same normalization build_db.py applies when it fills bill_mentions, so a
47	# user-typed "H.R. 5376" lands on the stored key "HR5376". Kept in sync by hand;
48	# if build_db.py's BILL_RE/norm_bill change, change these too.
49	BILL_RE = re.compile(
50	    r"\b("
51	    r"H\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|"
52	    r"H\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|"
53	    r"H\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*RES\.?|"
54	    r"H\.?\s*R\.?|S\.?"
55	    r")\s*(\d{1,5})\b",
56	    re.IGNORECASE,
57	)
58	
59	
60	def norm_bill(prefix, number):
61	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Z]", "", prefix.upper()) + number
62	
63	
64	def normalize_number(text):
65	    """Return the canonical bill token (e.g. 'HR5376') if `text` is a bill
66	    number, else None. Anchored so 'CHIPS Act' isn't read as a number."""
67	    m = BILL_RE.fullmatch(text.strip())
68	    return norm_bill(m.group(1), m.group(2)) if m else None
69	
70	
71	def congress_of_year(year):
72	    # 117th = 2021-22, 118th = 2023-24, 119th = 2025-26, ...
73	    return 117 + (int(year) - 2021) // 2
74	
75	
76	def phrase_pattern(phrases):
77	    """RE2 (DuckDB) whole-word, case-insensitive pattern over the phrase list.
78	    RE2 has no lookaround, so word boundaries use \\b; metacharacters escaped."""
79	    esc = [re.sub(r"([.^$*+?()\[\]{}|\\])", r"\\\1", p) for p in phrases]
80	    return r"(?i)\b(" + "|".join(esc) + r")\b"
81	
82	
83	# --------------------------------------------------------------- alias resolve
84	
85	def load_aliases(path):
86	    data = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
87	    return data["_meta"], data["aliases"]
88	
89	
90	def resolve(query, aliases):
91	    """Resolve the input into {bills, phrases, alias, kind, label}.
92	
93	    - a bill number -> that number (+ any alias whose crosswalk contains it, so
94	      the number query also gets the alias's name phrases for say-vs-pay)
95	    - otherwise -> alias lookup by name / id (exact, then unique substring)
96	    """
97	    num = normalize_number(query)
98	    if num:
99	        hit = next((a for a in aliases if num in a.get("bills", [])), None)
100	        return {
101	            "kind": "number",
102	            "bills": [num],
103	            "phrases": hit["phrases"] if hit else [],
104	            "alias": hit,
105	            "label": f"{num}" + (f'  ("{hit["names"][0]}")' if hit else ""),
106	        }
107	
108	    q = query.strip().lower()
109	    exact = [a for a in aliases
110	             if q == a["id"] or q in [n.lower() for n in a["names"]]]
111	    cands = exact or [a for a in aliases
112	                      if any(q in n.lower() for n in a["names"]) or q in a["id"]]
113	    if len(cands) == 1:
114	        a = cands[0]
115	        return {
116	            "kind": "alias",
117	            "bills": list(a.get("bills", [])),
118	            "phrases": list(a.get("phrases", [])),
119	            "alias": a,
120	            "label": f'{a["names"][0]}' + (f'  ({", ".join(a["bills"])})'
121	                                           if a.get("bills") else "  (phrase-primary)"),
122	        }
123	    if len(cands) > 1:
124	        names = "; ".join(f'{a["names"][0]} [{a["id"]}]' for a in cands)
125	        sys.exit(f'"{query}" is ambiguous across: {names}\nRe-run with the id.')
126	    avail = ", ".join(sorted(a["id"] for a in aliases))
127	    sys.exit(f'No bill number and no alias matched "{query}".\n'
128	             f"Pass a number (e.g. HR5376) or one of: {avail}")
129	
130	
131	# ------------------------------------------------------------------ DB queries
132	
133	def q(con, sql, params=None):
134	    rel = con.execute(sql, params or [])
135	    cols = [d[0] for d in rel.description]
136	    return [dict(zip(cols, r)) for r in rel.fetchall()]
137	
138	
139	def in_clause(bills):
140	    return "(" + ",".join("?" for _ in bills) + ")"
141	
142	
143	def senate_number(con, bills, top, limit):
144	    ph = in_clause(bills)
145	    dedup = f"""
146	      WITH keys AS (
147	        SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions
148	        WHERE dataset='senate' AND bill IN {ph}),
149	      f AS (
150	        SELECT sf.filing_uuid, sf.registrant_name, sf.client_name,
151	               sf.income, sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period, sf.posted
152	        FROM senate_filings sf JOIN keys ON sf.filing_uuid = keys.record_key
153	        QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
154	          PARTITION BY upper(trim(sf.registrant_name)), upper(trim(sf.client_name)),
155	                       sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period
156	          ORDER BY sf.posted DESC) = 1)"""
157	    raw = q(con, f"SELECT count(DISTINCT record_key) n FROM bill_mentions "
158	                 f"WHERE dataset='senate' AND bill IN {ph}", bills)[0]["n"]
159	    summ = q(con, dedup + " SELECT count(*) engagements, "
160	             "count(DISTINCT upper(trim(client_name))) clients, "
161	             "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income FROM f", bills)[0]
162	    years = q(con, dedup + " SELECT filing_year, count(*) n FROM f "
163	              "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1", bills)
164	    clients = q(con, dedup + " SELECT client_name, "
165	                "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income, count(*) filings FROM f "
166	                "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY attributed_income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?",
167	                bills + [top])
168	    sample = q(con, dedup + " SELECT filing_uuid record_key, client_name, "
169	               "registrant_name, income::BIGINT income, filing_year FROM f "
170	               "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", bills + [limit])
171	    return {"raw_mention_filings": raw, "summary": summ, "years": years,
172	            "top_clients": clients, "sample": sample}
173	
174	
175	def house_number(con, bills, top, limit):
176	    ph = in_clause(bills)
177	    base = f"""
178	      WITH keys AS (
179	        SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions
180	        WHERE dataset='house' AND bill IN {ph}),
181	      f AS (
182	        SELECT hf.filing_id, hf.organization_name, hf.client_name,
183	               hf.income, hf.report_year, hf.report_period
184	        FROM house_filings hf JOIN keys ON hf.filing_id = keys.record_key
185	        QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
186	          PARTITION BY upper(trim(hf.organization_name)), upper(trim(hf.client_name)),
187	                       hf.report_year, hf.report_period
188	          ORDER BY hf.filing_id DESC) = 1)"""
189	    summ = q(con, base + " SELECT count(*) filings, "
190	             "count(DISTINCT upper(trim(client_name))) clients, "
191	             "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income FROM f", bills)[0]
192	    years = q(con, base + " SELECT report_year, count(*) n FROM f "
193	              "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1", bills)
194	    clients = q(con, base + " SELECT client_name, organization_name, "
195	                "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income, count(*) filings FROM f "
196	                "GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY attributed_income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?",
197	                bills + [top])
198	    sample = q(con, base + " SELECT filing_id record_key, client_name, "
199	               "organization_name, income::BIGINT income, report_year FROM f "
200	               "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", bills + [limit])
201	    return {"summary": summ, "years": years, "top_clients": clients,
202	            "sample": sample}
203	
204	
205	def press_side(con, bills, phrases, top, limit):
206	    """Releases that cite the NUMBER and/or NAME the bill; the name-only set is
207	    the say-vs-pay coverage a number-only match would have missed."""
208	    num_keys, name_keys = [], []
209	    if bills:
210	        ph = in_clause(bills)
211	        num_keys = [r["record_key"] for r in q(
212	            con, f"SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions "
213	                 f"WHERE dataset='press' AND bill IN {ph}", bills)]
214	    if phrases:
215	        pat = phrase_pattern(phrases)
216	        name_keys = [r["k"] for r in q(
217	            con, "SELECT src_file || ':' || src_line k FROM press_releases "
218	                 "WHERE regexp_matches(text, ?)", [pat])]
219	    num_set, name_set = set(num_keys), set(name_keys)
220	    all_keys = num_set | name_set
221	    if not all_keys:
222	        return {"n_number": 0, "n_name": 0, "n_name_only": 0, "n_total": 0,
223	                "top_members": [], "years": [], "sample": []}
224	
225	    con.execute("CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP TABLE _pk(k TEXT, via TEXT)")
226	    con.executemany("INSERT INTO _pk VALUES (?, ?)",
227	                    [(k, "both" if k in num_set and k in name_set
228	                         else "number" if k in num_set else "name")
229	                     for k in all_keys])
230	    joined = """
231	      FROM _pk JOIN press_releases pr
232	        ON _pk.k = pr.src_file || ':' || pr.src_line"""
233	    top_members = q(con, "SELECT pr.member_name, pr.party, pr.state, "
234	                    "count(*) releases" + joined +
235	                    " GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY releases DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [top])
236	    years = q(con, "SELECT substr(pr.date,1,4) yr, count(*) n" + joined +
237	              " WHERE pr.date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1")
238	    sample = q(con, "SELECT _pk.k record_key, _pk.via, pr.member_name, "
239	               "pr.party, pr.state, pr.date, pr.title" + joined +
240	               " ORDER BY pr.date DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [limit])
241	    return {"n_number": len(num_set), "n_name": len(name_set),
242	            "n_name_only": len(name_set - num_set), "n_total": len(all_keys),
243	            "top_members": top_members, "years": years, "sample": sample}
244	
245	
246	def freetext_name(con, phrases, limit):
247	    """NAME-match filing free-text: the only filing-side signal for phrase-primary
248	    bills (Farm Bill), and an optional recall boost for numbered ones."""
249	    pat = phrase_pattern(phrases)
250	    sen = q(con, """
251	        WITH k AS (SELECT DISTINCT filing_uuid FROM senate_activities
252	                   WHERE regexp_matches(description, ?))
253	        SELECT count(*) n FROM k""", [pat])[0]["n"]
254	    sen_s = q(con, """
255	        WITH k AS (SELECT DISTINCT filing_uuid FROM senate_activities
256	                   WHERE regexp_matches(description, ?))
257	        SELECT sf.filing_uuid record_key, sf.client_name, sf.registrant_name,
258	               sf.income::BIGINT income, sf.filing_year
259	        FROM senate_filings sf JOIN k USING (filing_uuid)
260	        ORDER BY sf.income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?""", [pat, limit])
261	    hou = q(con, "SELECT count(DISTINCT filing_id) n FROM house_filings "
262	                 "WHERE regexp_matches(specific_issues, ?)", [pat])[0]["n"]
263	    hou_s = q(con, "SELECT filing_id record_key, client_name, organization_name, "
264	              "income::BIGINT income, report_year FROM house_filings "
265	              "WHERE regexp_matches(specific_issues, ?) "
266	              "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [pat, limit])
267	    return {"senate_filings": sen, "senate_sample": sen_s,
268	            "house_filings": hou, "house_sample": hou_s}
269	
270	
271	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- presentation
272	
273	def fmt_money(v):
274	    return f"${v:,}" if isinstance(v, int) else "·"
275	
276	
277	def render(res, r, datasets, meta, data_root, freetext, scanned_freetext):
278	    L = []
279	    a = r["alias"]
280	    L.append("=" * 78)
281	    L.append(f"BILL CROSS-CHECK  ·  {r['label']}")
282	    L.append("=" * 78)
283	    if a:
284	        L.append(f"alias id     : {a['id']}")
285	        if a.get("congress"):
286	            L.append(f"congress     : {a['congress']}"
287	                     + (f"   enacted {a['enacted']}  (P.L. {a.get('public_law','?')})"
288	                        if a.get("enacted") else ""))
289	        L.append(f"crosswalk    : {', '.join(a['bills']) if a['bills'] else '(none — phrase-primary)'}")
290	        L.append(f"match phrases: {', '.join(a['phrases']) if a['phrases'] else '(none)'}")
291	        L.append(f"note         : {a['note']}")
292	        L.append(f"source       : {a['source']}")
293	    elif r["phrases"]:
294	        L.append(f"match phrases: {', '.join(r['phrases'])}   (from crosswalk)")
295	    else:
296	        L.append("note         : bare number, no alias in the crosswalk — number "
297	                 "match only (add a bill_aliases.json entry to get name matching)")
298	    L.append("")
299	
300	    if "senate" in datasets and r["bills"]:
301	        s = res["senate_number"]
302	        su = s["summary"]
303	        cohorts = sorted({congress_of_year(y["filing_year"]) for y in s["years"]
304	                          if y["filing_year"]})
305	        L.append("── SENATE (number-cited) " + "─" * 53)
306	        L.append(f"  {su['engagements']} engagement-quarter filings "
307	                 f"(dedup of {s['raw_mention_filings']} raw) · "
308	                 f"{su['clients']} distinct clients · "
309	                 f"{fmt_money(su['attributed_income'])} attributed income*")
310	        L.append("  by filing year: " + ", ".join(
311	            f"{y['filing_year']}:{y['n']}" for y in s["years"]))
312	        # A single enacted law (IRA) referenced across years is implementation
313	        # lobbying of the SAME bill, not distinct bills — don't cry wolf. The
314	        # real reassignment risk is a bare/recurring number with no single
315	        # enacted anchor (H.R.1 each Congress; NDAA's per-FY numbers).
316	        single_enacted = bool(a and a.get("enacted") and a.get("congress")
317	                              and len(r["bills"]) == 1)
318	        if len(cohorts) > 1 and single_enacted:
319	            L.append(f"  ℹ referenced across {s['years'][0]['filing_year']}–"
320	                     f"{s['years'][-1]['filing_year']}: ongoing implementation "
321	                     f"lobbying of the enacted {a['congress']}th-Congress law, "
322	                     "not distinct bills.")
323	        elif len(cohorts) > 1:
324	            L.append(f"  ⚠ AMBIGUITY: mentions span Congresses {cohorts}. "
325	                     "bill_mentions has no Congress field, so this one number may")
326	            L.append("    denote a DIFFERENT bill each Congress (e.g. H.R.1 is a "
327	                     "new bill each term) — split by year before trusting a total.")
328	        for c in s["top_clients"][:8]:
329	            L.append(f"    {fmt_money(c['attributed_income']):>16}  "
330	                     f"{(c['client_name'] or '·')[:52]}")
331	        L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
332	        for x in s["sample"]:
333	            L.append(f"    {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
334	                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:44]}")
335	        L.append("")
336	
337	    if "house" in datasets and r["bills"]:
338	        h = res["house_number"]
339	        hu = h["summary"]
340	        L.append("── HOUSE (number-cited) " + "─" * 54)
341	        L.append(f"  {hu['filings']} filings · {hu['clients']} distinct clients · "
342	                 f"{fmt_money(hu['attributed_income'])} attributed income*  "
343	                 "(House XML is a partial snapshot — under-counts recent quarters)")
344	        L.append("  by report year: " + ", ".join(
345	            f"{y['report_year']}:{y['n']}" for y in h["years"]))
346	        for c in h["top_clients"][:8]:
347	            L.append(f"    {fmt_money(c['attributed_income']):>16}  "
348	                     f"{(c['client_name'] or '·')[:52]}")
349	        L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
350	        for x in h["sample"]:
351	            L.append(f"    {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
352	                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:44]}")
353	        L.append("")
354	
355	    if "press" in datasets:
356	        p = res["press"]
357	        L.append("── PRESS " + "─" * 69)
358	        if not r["phrases"] and not r["bills"]:
359	            L.append("  (nothing to match)")
360	        else:
361	            L.append(f"  {p['n_total']} distinct releases touch this bill:  "
362	                     f"{p['n_number']} cite the number · {p['n_name']} name it · "
363	                     f"{p['n_name_only']} name-only")
364	            if r["bills"] and r["phrases"] and p["n_name_only"] > p["n_number"]:
365	                L.append(f"  ⚡ SAY-vs-PAY: {p['n_name_only']} releases name this bill "
366	                         f"but never cite its number — invisible to number-only")
367	                L.append("    matching (the L004 trap). Name matching recovers them.")
368	            if p["years"]:
369	                L.append("  by year: " + ", ".join(
370	                    f"{y['yr']}:{y['n']}" for y in p["years"])
371	                    + "   (see the vintage caveat below)")
372	            for m in p["top_members"][:8]:
373	                L.append(f"    {(m['releases']):>5}×  {(m['member_name'] or '·')[:40]} "
374	                         f"({m['party'] or '?'}-{m['state'] or '?'})")
375	            L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
376	            for x in p["sample"]:
377	                L.append(f"    [{x['via']:>6}] {x['record_key']}  "
378	                         f"{(x['member_name'] or '·')[:22]}  {x['date'] or '·'}  "
379	                         f"{(x['title'] or '')[:36]}")
380	        L.append("")
381	
382	    if freetext and res.get("freetext"):
383	        ft = res["freetext"]
384	        why = "phrase-primary bill" if scanned_freetext == "auto" else "--scan-freetext"
385	        L.append(f"── FILING FREE-TEXT (name-matched; {why}) " + "─" * 33)
386	        L.append(f"  senate: {ft['senate_filings']} filings whose activity text "
387	                 f"names it · house: {ft['house_filings']} filings")
388	        for x in ft["senate_sample"][:5]:
389	            L.append(f"    [senate] {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
390	                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:40]}")
391	        for x in ft["house_sample"][:5]:
392	            L.append(f"    [house ] {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
393	                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:40]}")
394	        L.append("")
395	
396	    L.append("* attributed income is FILING-LEVEL: a filing naming several bills "
397	             "attributes its full income to each — a ranking signal, not exact")
398	    L.append("  dollars. Senate/House are reported separately (never summed: LD-2s "
399	             "are filed with both chambers). For exact client dollars feed the")
400	    L.append("  client into lda-entity-resolver's v_client_canonical_spend (P1).")
401	    L.append("* Press counts are RAW and VINTAGE-SENSITIVE: the press corpus starts "
402	             "2022-01-01 and grows ~4x by 2025 (19.7k→48.3k releases/yr), and a bill's")
403	    L.append("  press attention concentrates in its brief legislative window. So an "
404	             "early-vintage bill (window in the thin 2022 corpus, and any pre-2022")
405	    L.append("  advocacy unseen) shows far fewer name-matches than a 2025-era one — use "
406	             "the by-year facet to see WHO named it and WHEN, not to rank bills by loudness.")
407	    L.append(f"  Resolve any key:  python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/show_record.py "
408	             f"<key> --data-root {data_root} --db db/lda_full.duckdb")
409	    L.append("  Citeable aggregate form: queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql")
410	    return "\n".join(L)
411	
412	
413	def main():
414	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
415	                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
416	    ap.add_argument("query", nargs="?")
417	    ap.add_argument("--db", default="db/lda_full.duckdb")
418	    ap.add_argument("--aliases",
419	                    default=str(Path(__file__).with_name("bill_aliases.json")))
420	    ap.add_argument("--dataset", default="senate,house,press")
421	    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=8)
422	    ap.add_argument("--top", type=int, default=10)
423	    ap.add_argument("--scan-freetext", action="store_true")
424	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", default="../data/data")
425	    ap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
426	    ap.add_argument("--list-aliases", action="store_true")
427	    args = ap.parse_args()
428	
429	    meta, aliases = load_aliases(args.aliases)
430	    if args.list_aliases:
431	        print(f"bill_aliases.json v{meta['version']} ({meta['generated']}) — "
432	              f"{len(aliases)} entries\n")
433	        for a in aliases:
434	            print(f"  {a['id']:38} {', '.join(a['bills']) or '(phrase-primary)':22} "
435	                  f"{a['names'][0]}")
436	        return
437	    if not args.query:
438	        ap.error("a bill number or alias is required (or use --list-aliases)")
439	
440	    datasets = [d.strip() for d in args.dataset.split(",") if d.strip()]
441	    r = resolve(args.query, aliases)
442	    con = duckdb.connect(args.db, read_only=True)
443	
444	    res = {}
445	    if "senate" in datasets and r["bills"]:
446	        res["senate_number"] = senate_number(con, r["bills"], args.top, args.limit)
447	    if "house" in datasets and r["bills"]:
448	        res["house_number"] = house_number(con, r["bills"], args.top, args.limit)
449	    if "press" in datasets:
450	        res["press"] = press_side(con, r["bills"], r["phrases"], args.top, args.limit)
451	
452	    # Filing free-text name match: auto for phrase-primary bills, else opt-in.
453	    scanned = None
454	    if r["phrases"]:
455	        if not r["bills"]:
456	            scanned = "auto"
457	        elif args.scan_freetext:
458	            scanned = "flag"
459	    if scanned:
460	        res["freetext"] = freetext_name(con, r["phrases"], args.limit)
461	
462	    if args.json:
463	        print(json.dumps({"query": args.query, "resolved": {
464	            "kind": r["kind"], "bills": r["bills"], "phrases": r["phrases"],
465	            "alias_id": r["alias"]["id"] if r["alias"] else None},
466	            "results": res}, indent=2, default=str))
467	    else:
468	        print(render(res, r, datasets, meta, args.data_root,
469	                     bool(scanned), scanned))
470	    con.close()
471	
472	
473	if __name__ == "__main__":
474	    main()
475

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Bill cross-check: given a bill (number OR named alias), list every Senate
filing, House filing, and press release that touches it, each with a citation
key resolvable by lda-corpus-loader/show_record.py.

Why this exists (the L004 lesson). Press releases and members name bills ("the
Farm Bill", "NDAA", "Inflation Reduction Act"); LDA filings cite H.R./S. numbers.
Matching on numbers alone fabricates "lobbied but publicly silent" bills. This
tool bridges the two: a query by number also surfaces the name-cited press (and,
for phrase-primary bills, the name-cited filings), and a query by name reaches
the number-cited filings via the curated crosswalk in bill_aliases.json.

  Number side  -> exact match on the pre-extracted `bill_mentions` table
  Name side    -> whole-word regex on press text (+ filing free-text on request)

Usage:
  python bill_lookup.py <bill-or-alias> [options]
    <bill-or-alias>   "HR5376" | "H.R. 5376" | "Inflation Reduction Act" | "Farm Bill"
  --db PATH           DuckDB (default db/lda_full.duckdb)
  --aliases PATH      alias table (default: bill_aliases.json beside this script)
  --dataset SET       senate,house,press  (comma list; default all)
  --limit N           sample record keys shown per dataset/mode (default 8)
  --top N             top clients/members ranked (default 10)
  --scan-freetext     also NAME-match filing free-text (senate activity
                      descriptions, house specific_issues). Auto-on for
                      phrase-primary bills (no reliable number, e.g. Farm Bill).
  --data-root PATH    printed into the show_record.py hint (default ../../../data/data)
  --json              machine-readable output
  --list-aliases      print the alias table and exit

Aggregate counts/rankings this prints are also expressible as a labeled block in
queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql (the citeable form for findings).
"""

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import duckdb

if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")

# Same normalization build_db.py applies when it fills bill_mentions, so a
# user-typed "H.R. 5376" lands on the stored key "HR5376". Kept in sync by hand;
# if build_db.py's BILL_RE/norm_bill change, change these too.
BILL_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b("
    r"H\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*RES\.?|"
    r"H\.?\s*R\.?|S\.?"
    r")\s*(\d{1,5})\b",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)


def norm_bill(prefix, number):
    return re.sub(r"[^A-Z]", "", prefix.upper()) + number


def normalize_number(text):
    """Return the canonical bill token (e.g. 'HR5376') if `text` is a bill
    number, else None. Anchored so 'CHIPS Act' isn't read as a number."""
    m = BILL_RE.fullmatch(text.strip())
    return norm_bill(m.group(1), m.group(2)) if m else None


def congress_of_year(year):
    # 117th = 2021-22, 118th = 2023-24, 119th = 2025-26, ...
    return 117 + (int(year) - 2021) // 2


def phrase_pattern(phrases):
    """RE2 (DuckDB) whole-word, case-insensitive pattern over the phrase list.
    RE2 has no lookaround, so word boundaries use \\b; metacharacters escaped."""
    esc = [re.sub(r"([.^$*+?()\[\]{}|\\])", r"\\\1", p) for p in phrases]
    return r"(?i)\b(" + "|".join(esc) + r")\b"


# --------------------------------------------------------------- alias resolve

def load_aliases(path):
    data = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return data["_meta"], data["aliases"]


def resolve(query, aliases):
    """Resolve the input into {bills, phrases, alias, kind, label}.

    - a bill number -> that number (+ any alias whose crosswalk contains it, so
      the number query also gets the alias's name phrases for say-vs-pay)
    - otherwise -> alias lookup by name / id (exact, then unique substring)
    """
    num = normalize_number(query)
    if num:
        hit = next((a for a in aliases if num in a.get("bills", [])), None)
        return {
            "kind": "number",
            "bills": [num],
            "phrases": hit["phrases"] if hit else [],
            "alias": hit,
            "label": f"{num}" + (f'  ("{hit["names"][0]}")' if hit else ""),
        }

    q = query.strip().lower()
    exact = [a for a in aliases
             if q == a["id"] or q in [n.lower() for n in a["names"]]]
    cands = exact or [a for a in aliases
                      if any(q in n.lower() for n in a["names"]) or q in a["id"]]
    if len(cands) == 1:
        a = cands[0]
        return {
            "kind": "alias",
            "bills": list(a.get("bills", [])),
            "phrases": list(a.get("phrases", [])),
            "alias": a,
            "label": f'{a["names"][0]}' + (f'  ({", ".join(a["bills"])})'
                                           if a.get("bills") else "  (phrase-primary)"),
        }
    if len(cands) > 1:
        names = "; ".join(f'{a["names"][0]} [{a["id"]}]' for a in cands)
        sys.exit(f'"{query}" is ambiguous across: {names}\nRe-run with the id.')
    avail = ", ".join(sorted(a["id"] for a in aliases))
    sys.exit(f'No bill number and no alias matched "{query}".\n'
             f"Pass a number (e.g. HR5376) or one of: {avail}")


# ------------------------------------------------------------------ DB queries

def q(con, sql, params=None):
    rel = con.execute(sql, params or [])
    cols = [d[0] for d in rel.description]
    return [dict(zip(cols, r)) for r in rel.fetchall()]


def in_clause(bills):
    return "(" + ",".join("?" for _ in bills) + ")"


def senate_number(con, bills, top, limit):
    ph = in_clause(bills)
    dedup = f"""
      WITH keys AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions
        WHERE dataset='senate' AND bill IN {ph}),
      f AS (
        SELECT sf.filing_uuid, sf.registrant_name, sf.client_name,
               sf.income, sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period, sf.posted
        FROM senate_filings sf JOIN keys ON sf.filing_uuid = keys.record_key
        QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
          PARTITION BY upper(trim(sf.registrant_name)), upper(trim(sf.client_name)),
                       sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period
          ORDER BY sf.posted DESC) = 1)"""
    raw = q(con, f"SELECT count(DISTINCT record_key) n FROM bill_mentions "
                 f"WHERE dataset='senate' AND bill IN {ph}", bills)[0]["n"]
    summ = q(con, dedup + " SELECT count(*) engagements, "
             "count(DISTINCT upper(trim(client_name))) clients, "
             "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income FROM f", bills)[0]
    years = q(con, dedup + " SELECT filing_year, count(*) n FROM f "
              "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1", bills)
    clients = q(con, dedup + " SELECT client_name, "
                "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income, count(*) filings FROM f "
                "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY attributed_income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?",
                bills + [top])
    sample = q(con, dedup + " SELECT filing_uuid record_key, client_name, "
               "registrant_name, income::BIGINT income, filing_year FROM f "
               "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", bills + [limit])
    return {"raw_mention_filings": raw, "summary": summ, "years": years,
            "top_clients": clients, "sample": sample}


def house_number(con, bills, top, limit):
    ph = in_clause(bills)
    base = f"""
      WITH keys AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions
        WHERE dataset='house' AND bill IN {ph}),
      f AS (
        SELECT hf.filing_id, hf.organization_name, hf.client_name,
               hf.income, hf.report_year, hf.report_period
        FROM house_filings hf JOIN keys ON hf.filing_id = keys.record_key
        QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
          PARTITION BY upper(trim(hf.organization_name)), upper(trim(hf.client_name)),
                       hf.report_year, hf.report_period
          ORDER BY hf.filing_id DESC) = 1)"""
    summ = q(con, base + " SELECT count(*) filings, "
             "count(DISTINCT upper(trim(client_name))) clients, "
             "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income FROM f", bills)[0]
    years = q(con, base + " SELECT report_year, count(*) n FROM f "
              "GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1", bills)
    clients = q(con, base + " SELECT client_name, organization_name, "
                "sum(income)::BIGINT attributed_income, count(*) filings FROM f "
                "GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY attributed_income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?",
                bills + [top])
    sample = q(con, base + " SELECT filing_id record_key, client_name, "
               "organization_name, income::BIGINT income, report_year FROM f "
               "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", bills + [limit])
    return {"summary": summ, "years": years, "top_clients": clients,
            "sample": sample}


def press_side(con, bills, phrases, top, limit):
    """Releases that cite the NUMBER and/or NAME the bill; the name-only set is
    the say-vs-pay coverage a number-only match would have missed."""
    num_keys, name_keys = [], []
    if bills:
        ph = in_clause(bills)
        num_keys = [r["record_key"] for r in q(
            con, f"SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions "
                 f"WHERE dataset='press' AND bill IN {ph}", bills)]
    if phrases:
        pat = phrase_pattern(phrases)
        name_keys = [r["k"] for r in q(
            con, "SELECT src_file || ':' || src_line k FROM press_releases "
                 "WHERE regexp_matches(text, ?)", [pat])]
    num_set, name_set = set(num_keys), set(name_keys)
    all_keys = num_set | name_set
    if not all_keys:
        return {"n_number": 0, "n_name": 0, "n_name_only": 0, "n_total": 0,
                "top_members": [], "years": [], "sample": []}

    con.execute("CREATE OR REPLACE TEMP TABLE _pk(k TEXT, via TEXT)")
    con.executemany("INSERT INTO _pk VALUES (?, ?)",
                    [(k, "both" if k in num_set and k in name_set
                         else "number" if k in num_set else "name")
                     for k in all_keys])
    joined = """
      FROM _pk JOIN press_releases pr
        ON _pk.k = pr.src_file || ':' || pr.src_line"""
    top_members = q(con, "SELECT pr.member_name, pr.party, pr.state, "
                    "count(*) releases" + joined +
                    " GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY releases DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [top])
    years = q(con, "SELECT substr(pr.date,1,4) yr, count(*) n" + joined +
              " WHERE pr.date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1")
    sample = q(con, "SELECT _pk.k record_key, _pk.via, pr.member_name, "
               "pr.party, pr.state, pr.date, pr.title" + joined +
               " ORDER BY pr.date DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [limit])
    return {"n_number": len(num_set), "n_name": len(name_set),
            "n_name_only": len(name_set - num_set), "n_total": len(all_keys),
            "top_members": top_members, "years": years, "sample": sample}


def freetext_name(con, phrases, limit):
    """NAME-match filing free-text: the only filing-side signal for phrase-primary
    bills (Farm Bill), and an optional recall boost for numbered ones."""
    pat = phrase_pattern(phrases)
    sen = q(con, """
        WITH k AS (SELECT DISTINCT filing_uuid FROM senate_activities
                   WHERE regexp_matches(description, ?))
        SELECT count(*) n FROM k""", [pat])[0]["n"]
    sen_s = q(con, """
        WITH k AS (SELECT DISTINCT filing_uuid FROM senate_activities
                   WHERE regexp_matches(description, ?))
        SELECT sf.filing_uuid record_key, sf.client_name, sf.registrant_name,
               sf.income::BIGINT income, sf.filing_year
        FROM senate_filings sf JOIN k USING (filing_uuid)
        ORDER BY sf.income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?""", [pat, limit])
    hou = q(con, "SELECT count(DISTINCT filing_id) n FROM house_filings "
                 "WHERE regexp_matches(specific_issues, ?)", [pat])[0]["n"]
    hou_s = q(con, "SELECT filing_id record_key, client_name, organization_name, "
              "income::BIGINT income, report_year FROM house_filings "
              "WHERE regexp_matches(specific_issues, ?) "
              "ORDER BY income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT ?", [pat, limit])
    return {"senate_filings": sen, "senate_sample": sen_s,
            "house_filings": hou, "house_sample": hou_s}


# ----------------------------------------------------------------- presentation

def fmt_money(v):
    return f"${v:,}" if isinstance(v, int) else "·"


def render(res, r, datasets, meta, data_root, freetext, scanned_freetext):
    L = []
    a = r["alias"]
    L.append("=" * 78)
    L.append(f"BILL CROSS-CHECK  ·  {r['label']}")
    L.append("=" * 78)
    if a:
        L.append(f"alias id     : {a['id']}")
        if a.get("congress"):
            L.append(f"congress     : {a['congress']}"
                     + (f"   enacted {a['enacted']}  (P.L. {a.get('public_law','?')})"
                        if a.get("enacted") else ""))
        L.append(f"crosswalk    : {', '.join(a['bills']) if a['bills'] else '(none — phrase-primary)'}")
        L.append(f"match phrases: {', '.join(a['phrases']) if a['phrases'] else '(none)'}")
        L.append(f"note         : {a['note']}")
        L.append(f"source       : {a['source']}")
    elif r["phrases"]:
        L.append(f"match phrases: {', '.join(r['phrases'])}   (from crosswalk)")
    else:
        L.append("note         : bare number, no alias in the crosswalk — number "
                 "match only (add a bill_aliases.json entry to get name matching)")
    L.append("")

    if "senate" in datasets and r["bills"]:
        s = res["senate_number"]
        su = s["summary"]
        cohorts = sorted({congress_of_year(y["filing_year"]) for y in s["years"]
                          if y["filing_year"]})
        L.append("── SENATE (number-cited) " + "─" * 53)
        L.append(f"  {su['engagements']} engagement-quarter filings "
                 f"(dedup of {s['raw_mention_filings']} raw) · "
                 f"{su['clients']} distinct clients · "
                 f"{fmt_money(su['attributed_income'])} attributed income*")
        L.append("  by filing year: " + ", ".join(
            f"{y['filing_year']}:{y['n']}" for y in s["years"]))
        # A single enacted law (IRA) referenced across years is implementation
        # lobbying of the SAME bill, not distinct bills — don't cry wolf. The
        # real reassignment risk is a bare/recurring number with no single
        # enacted anchor (H.R.1 each Congress; NDAA's per-FY numbers).
        single_enacted = bool(a and a.get("enacted") and a.get("congress")
                              and len(r["bills"]) == 1)
        if len(cohorts) > 1 and single_enacted:
            L.append(f"  ℹ referenced across {s['years'][0]['filing_year']}–"
                     f"{s['years'][-1]['filing_year']}: ongoing implementation "
                     f"lobbying of the enacted {a['congress']}th-Congress law, "
                     "not distinct bills.")
        elif len(cohorts) > 1:
            L.append(f"  ⚠ AMBIGUITY: mentions span Congresses {cohorts}. "
                     "bill_mentions has no Congress field, so this one number may")
            L.append("    denote a DIFFERENT bill each Congress (e.g. H.R.1 is a "
                     "new bill each term) — split by year before trusting a total.")
        for c in s["top_clients"][:8]:
            L.append(f"    {fmt_money(c['attributed_income']):>16}  "
                     f"{(c['client_name'] or '·')[:52]}")
        L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
        for x in s["sample"]:
            L.append(f"    {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:44]}")
        L.append("")

    if "house" in datasets and r["bills"]:
        h = res["house_number"]
        hu = h["summary"]
        L.append("── HOUSE (number-cited) " + "─" * 54)
        L.append(f"  {hu['filings']} filings · {hu['clients']} distinct clients · "
                 f"{fmt_money(hu['attributed_income'])} attributed income*  "
                 "(House XML is a partial snapshot — under-counts recent quarters)")
        L.append("  by report year: " + ", ".join(
            f"{y['report_year']}:{y['n']}" for y in h["years"]))
        for c in h["top_clients"][:8]:
            L.append(f"    {fmt_money(c['attributed_income']):>16}  "
                     f"{(c['client_name'] or '·')[:52]}")
        L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
        for x in h["sample"]:
            L.append(f"    {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:44]}")
        L.append("")

    if "press" in datasets:
        p = res["press"]
        L.append("── PRESS " + "─" * 69)
        if not r["phrases"] and not r["bills"]:
            L.append("  (nothing to match)")
        else:
            L.append(f"  {p['n_total']} distinct releases touch this bill:  "
                     f"{p['n_number']} cite the number · {p['n_name']} name it · "
                     f"{p['n_name_only']} name-only")
            if r["bills"] and r["phrases"] and p["n_name_only"] > p["n_number"]:
                L.append(f"  ⚡ SAY-vs-PAY: {p['n_name_only']} releases name this bill "
                         f"but never cite its number — invisible to number-only")
                L.append("    matching (the L004 trap). Name matching recovers them.")
            if p["years"]:
                L.append("  by year: " + ", ".join(
                    f"{y['yr']}:{y['n']}" for y in p["years"])
                    + "   (see the vintage caveat below)")
            for m in p["top_members"][:8]:
                L.append(f"    {(m['releases']):>5}×  {(m['member_name'] or '·')[:40]} "
                         f"({m['party'] or '?'}-{m['state'] or '?'})")
            L.append("  sample keys (show_record.py):")
            for x in p["sample"]:
                L.append(f"    [{x['via']:>6}] {x['record_key']}  "
                         f"{(x['member_name'] or '·')[:22]}  {x['date'] or '·'}  "
                         f"{(x['title'] or '')[:36]}")
        L.append("")

    if freetext and res.get("freetext"):
        ft = res["freetext"]
        why = "phrase-primary bill" if scanned_freetext == "auto" else "--scan-freetext"
        L.append(f"── FILING FREE-TEXT (name-matched; {why}) " + "─" * 33)
        L.append(f"  senate: {ft['senate_filings']} filings whose activity text "
                 f"names it · house: {ft['house_filings']} filings")
        for x in ft["senate_sample"][:5]:
            L.append(f"    [senate] {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:40]}")
        for x in ft["house_sample"][:5]:
            L.append(f"    [house ] {x['record_key']}  {fmt_money(x['income']):>14}  "
                     f"{(x['client_name'] or '·')[:40]}")
        L.append("")

    L.append("* attributed income is FILING-LEVEL: a filing naming several bills "
             "attributes its full income to each — a ranking signal, not exact")
    L.append("  dollars. Senate/House are reported separately (never summed: LD-2s "
             "are filed with both chambers). For exact client dollars feed the")
    L.append("  client into lda-entity-resolver's v_client_canonical_spend (P1).")
    L.append("* Press counts are RAW and VINTAGE-SENSITIVE: the press corpus starts "
             "2022-01-01 and grows ~4x by 2025 (19.7k→48.3k releases/yr), and a bill's")
    L.append("  press attention concentrates in its brief legislative window. So an "
             "early-vintage bill (window in the thin 2022 corpus, and any pre-2022")
    L.append("  advocacy unseen) shows far fewer name-matches than a 2025-era one — use "
             "the by-year facet to see WHO named it and WHEN, not to rank bills by loudness.")
    L.append(f"  Resolve any key:  python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/show_record.py "
             f"<key> --data-root {data_root} --db db/lda_full.duckdb")
    L.append("  Citeable aggregate form: queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql")
    return "\n".join(L)


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
    ap.add_argument("query", nargs="?")
    ap.add_argument("--db", default="db/lda_full.duckdb")
    ap.add_argument("--aliases",
                    default=str(Path(__file__).with_name("bill_aliases.json")))
    ap.add_argument("--dataset", default="senate,house,press")
    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=8)
    ap.add_argument("--top", type=int, default=10)
    ap.add_argument("--scan-freetext", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", default="../data/data")
    ap.add_argument("--json", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--list-aliases", action="store_true")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    meta, aliases = load_aliases(args.aliases)
    if args.list_aliases:
        print(f"bill_aliases.json v{meta['version']} ({meta['generated']}) — "
              f"{len(aliases)} entries\n")
        for a in aliases:
            print(f"  {a['id']:38} {', '.join(a['bills']) or '(phrase-primary)':22} "
                  f"{a['names'][0]}")
        return
    if not args.query:
        ap.error("a bill number or alias is required (or use --list-aliases)")

    datasets = [d.strip() for d in args.dataset.split(",") if d.strip()]
    r = resolve(args.query, aliases)
    con = duckdb.connect(args.db, read_only=True)

    res = {}
    if "senate" in datasets and r["bills"]:
        res["senate_number"] = senate_number(con, r["bills"], args.top, args.limit)
    if "house" in datasets and r["bills"]:
        res["house_number"] = house_number(con, r["bills"], args.top, args.limit)
    if "press" in datasets:
        res["press"] = press_side(con, r["bills"], r["phrases"], args.top, args.limit)

    # Filing free-text name match: auto for phrase-primary bills, else opt-in.
    scanned = None
    if r["phrases"]:
        if not r["bills"]:
            scanned = "auto"
        elif args.scan_freetext:
            scanned = "flag"
    if scanned:
        res["freetext"] = freetext_name(con, r["phrases"], args.limit)

    if args.json:
        print(json.dumps({"query": args.query, "resolved": {
            "kind": r["kind"], "bills": r["bills"], "phrases": r["phrases"],
            "alias_id": r["alias"]["id"] if r["alias"] else None},
            "results": res}, indent=2, default=str))
    else:
        print(render(res, r, datasets, meta, args.data_root,
                     bool(scanned), scanned))
    con.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

118	    },

122	      "phrases": ["secure 2.0", "securing a strong retirement act"],

The House standalone vehicle was H.R.2954 (Securing a Strong Retirement Act of 2022). Because H.R.2617 is a broad omnibus, a number match on HR2617 is NOT specific to SECURE 2.0 retirement provisions; treat HR2617 hits as omnibus lobbying and lean on the name phrases. Medium confidence on completeness of vehicles.",

FY2022 = S.1605 (117th, P.L. 117-81); FY2023 = H.R.7776 (117th, P.L. 117-263); FY2024 = H.R.2670 (118th, P.L. 118-31); FY2025 = H.R.5009 (118th, P.L. 118-159). A number match here mixes fiscal years; use the year facets in bill_lookup.py output to separate them.",

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Build cross-dataset entity tables for the GAIN lobbying database.
3	
4	Creates three tables and one view inside an existing loader-built DuckDB:
5	
6	  entities           one row per resolved entity (registrant / client / foreign_entity)
7	  entity_aliases     every raw name variant -> entity, with a sample raw-record pointer
8	  registrant_crosswalk
9	                     senate registrant+client engagement -> matched house filings,
10	                     via the House <senateID> compound key
11	                     "<senate_registrant_id>-<senate_client_id>" (verified against real
12	                     records 2026-07-06; sampled pairs agree on names). ID matches are
13	                     labeled confidence='id'; there is no fuzzy tier in this pass —
14	                     name-only candidates are reported, never silently merged.
15	  v_client_canonical_spend (VIEW)
16	                     per-client, per-quarter senate lobbying spend with the in-house
17	                     rollup double-count removed (P1). See CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW below
18	                     for the rule; queries/p1_canonical_spend.sql for cited demos/QA.
19	
20	Normalization (deterministic, in `norm_name` below): uppercase, strip whitespace,
21	drop parenthetical qualifiers ("MUBADALA INVESTMENT COMPANY (OWNS 24% OF CATURUS)"
22	-> "MUBADALA INVESTMENT COMPANY"), collapse punctuation to spaces, strip legal
23	suffixes (LLC/INC/LTD/...) repeatedly from the tail. The normalized key is stored on
24	every alias row so any grouping decision can be audited in SQL.
25	
26	Senate-side entities carry their LDA numeric ids; House-side organizations (no UUIDs)
27	attach to senate entities only through the compound-key crosswalk, otherwise they
28	stay unresolved rather than fuzzy-merged (ambiguity is a report, not a merge).
29	
30	Usage:
31	  python resolve_entities.py --db db/lda_pilot.duckdb            # build tables
32	  python resolve_entities.py --db db/lda_pilot.duckdb --report   # QA report only
33	"""
34	
35	import argparse
36	import json
37	import os
38	import re
39	import sys
40	import tempfile
41	
42	import duckdb
43	
44	if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
45	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")
46	
47	LEGAL_SUFFIXES = {
48	    "LLC", "L L C", "INC", "INCORPORATED", "LTD", "LIMITED", "LLP", "L L P",
49	    "LP", "L P", "PLC", "CO", "CORP", "CORPORATION", "COMPANY", "PC", "PLLC",
50	    "SA", "S A", "AG", "GMBH", "NV", "N V", "BV", "B V", "PTY", "USA", "US",
51	}
52	
53	
54	def norm_name(raw):
55	    """Deterministic normalization key. Documented behavior; see module docstring."""
56	    if not raw:
57	        return None
58	    s = raw.upper().strip()
59	    s = re.sub(r"\([^)]*\)", " ", s)          # parenthetical qualifiers
60	    s = re.sub(r"[^A-Z0-9]+", " ", s).strip()  # punctuation -> spaces
61	    words = s.split()
62	    while words and words[-1] in LEGAL_SUFFIXES:
63	        words.pop()
64	    return " ".join(words) or None
65	
66	
67	DDL = """
68	CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE entities (
69	  entity_id TEXT, kind TEXT, canonical_name TEXT, norm_key TEXT,
70	  senate_id TEXT, n_aliases INTEGER, n_records INTEGER, sample_record TEXT);
71	
72	CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE entity_aliases (
73	  entity_id TEXT, kind TEXT, raw_name TEXT, norm_key TEXT, dataset TEXT,
74	  senate_id TEXT, n_records INTEGER, sample_record TEXT);
75	
76	CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE registrant_crosswalk (
77	  senate_registrant_id TEXT, senate_client_id TEXT,
78	  senate_registrant_name TEXT, senate_client_name TEXT,
79	  n_senate_filings INTEGER, n_house_filings INTEGER,
80	  sample_senate_uuid TEXT, sample_house_filing_id TEXT, confidence TEXT);
81	"""
82	
83	# One source per (kind, dataset): raw name, senate numeric id when the dataset has
84	# one, and a raw-record pointer sample. Foreign entities have no LDA id -> norm key
85	# only.
86	SOURCES = {
87	    ("registrant", "senate"): """
88	      SELECT registrant_name AS raw_name, registrant_id AS senate_id,
89	             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
90	      FROM senate_filings WHERE registrant_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
91	    ("client", "senate"): """
92	      SELECT client_name AS raw_name, client_id AS senate_id,
93	             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
94	      FROM senate_filings WHERE client_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
95	    ("registrant", "house"): """
96	      SELECT organization_name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
97	             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_id) AS sample_record
98	      FROM house_filings WHERE organization_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
99	    ("client", "house"): """
100	      SELECT client_name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
101	             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_id) AS sample_record
102	      FROM house_filings WHERE client_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
103	    ("foreign_entity", "senate"): """
104	      SELECT name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
105	             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
106	      FROM senate_foreign_entities WHERE name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
107	}
108	
109	ALIAS_COLS = ["entity_id", "kind", "raw_name", "norm_key", "dataset",
110	              "senate_id", "n_records", "sample_record"]
111	
112	# P1 — canonical client spend (rollup-correctness). A client that self-files
113	# (registrant norm_key == client norm_key, i.e. in-house lobbying) reports its TOTAL
114	# lobbying under that filing, which already subsumes the income the outside firms it
115	# hired report for the same client. Summing every filing for a client therefore
116	# double-counts. Per (client, quarter): canonical = greatest(in-house amount, outside
117	# amount) — NEVER their sum; both components + the naive sum-all + the delta are kept
118	# so any figure is auditable. `amount` is the row's reported figure regardless of the
119	# income/expenses field (some in-house filers report under `income`, not `expenses` —
120	# keying on `expenses` alone zeroed out real spenders like AIPAC). Amendments are
121	# deduped on filing_period (latest by `posted`), the same rule as sweep_2026.sql#H1c.
122	# Senate-only (the completeness-primary dataset; never sum senate+house). Some clients
123	# roll up and some don't — has_inhouse_filing + method make the branch explicit.
124	# NOTE: keep this in sync with queries/p1_canonical_spend.sql's header/demos.
125	CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW = """
126	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_client_canonical_spend AS
127	WITH rk AS (SELECT DISTINCT raw_name, norm_key FROM entity_aliases
128	            WHERE kind='registrant' AND dataset='senate'),
129	     ck AS (SELECT DISTINCT raw_name, norm_key FROM entity_aliases
130	            WHERE kind='client' AND dataset='senate'),
131	base AS (
132	  SELECT f.registrant_id, f.client_id, f.filing_year, f.filing_period, f.posted,
133	         greatest(coalesce(f.income, 0), coalesce(f.expenses, 0)) AS amount,
134	         rk.norm_key AS reg_key, ck.norm_key AS cli_key
135	  FROM senate_filings f
136	  LEFT JOIN rk ON rk.raw_name = f.registrant_name
137	  LEFT JOIN ck ON ck.raw_name = f.client_name
138	  WHERE f.filing_period IS NOT NULL
139	    AND (f.income IS NOT NULL OR f.expenses IS NOT NULL)
140	    AND ck.norm_key IS NOT NULL),
141	dedup AS (
142	  SELECT * FROM base QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
143	    PARTITION BY registrant_id, client_id, filing_year, filing_period
144	    ORDER BY posted DESC) = 1),
145	tagged AS (
146	  SELECT *, (reg_key IS NOT NULL AND reg_key = cli_key) AS self_filed FROM dedup),
147	agg AS (
148	  SELECT cli_key, filing_year, filing_period,
149	         bool_or(self_filed) AS has_inhouse_filing,
150	         coalesce(sum(amount) FILTER (WHERE self_filed), 0) AS inhouse_amount,
151	         coalesce(sum(amount) FILTER (WHERE NOT self_filed), 0) AS outside_amount,
152	         count(*) AS n_filings
153	  FROM tagged GROUP BY cli_key, filing_year, filing_period)
154	SELECT e.entity_id AS client_entity_id,
155	       coalesce(e.canonical_name, a.cli_key) AS client_name,
156	       a.cli_key AS client_norm_key, a.filing_year, a.filing_period,
157	       a.has_inhouse_filing, a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount,
158	       greatest(a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount) AS canonical_spend,
159	       a.inhouse_amount + a.outside_amount AS naive_sum_all,
160	       least(a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount) AS double_count_delta,
161	       CASE WHEN a.has_inhouse_filing AND a.inhouse_amount >= a.outside_amount
162	              THEN 'in-house total (outside firms subsumed)'
163	            WHEN a.has_inhouse_filing
164	              THEN 'outside sum (in-house filing under-reported)'
165	            ELSE 'outside sum (no in-house filing)' END AS method,
166	       a.n_filings
167	FROM agg a LEFT JOIN entities e ON e.kind = 'client' AND e.norm_key = a.cli_key
168	"""
169	
170	
171	def build(con):
172	    for stmt in DDL.split(";"):
173	        if stmt.strip():
174	            con.execute(stmt)
175	
176	    # Normalize in Python (no numpy/UDF dependency); stage via NDJSON + COPY,
177	    # same pattern as the loader's Sink.
178	    rows = []
179	    for (kind, dataset), sql in SOURCES.items():
180	        for raw_name, senate_id, n_records, sample in con.execute(sql).fetchall():
181	            key = norm_name(raw_name)
182	            if key:
183	                rows.append(dict(zip(ALIAS_COLS, (
184	                    None, kind, raw_name, key, dataset,
185	                    senate_id, n_records, sample))))
186	    fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".ndjson")
187	    os.close(fd)
188	    try:
189	        with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
190	            for r in rows:
191	                f.write(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False, default=str) + "\n")
192	        con.execute(
193	            f"COPY entity_aliases FROM '{tmp.replace(chr(92), '/')}' (FORMAT json)")
194	    finally:
195	        os.unlink(tmp)
196	
197	    # Entity id: senate numeric id where any alias in the (kind, norm_key) group
198	    # carries one (senate ids are authoritative), else the norm key. Kind is part of
199	    # the id so a firm that is both registrant and client stays two entities (they
200	    # play different roles; the crosswalk join is what links engagements).
201	    con.execute("""
202	      INSERT INTO entities
203	      WITH grouped AS (
204	        SELECT kind, norm_key, min(senate_id) AS senate_id,
205	               arg_max(raw_name, n_records) AS canonical_name,
206	               count(*) AS n_aliases, sum(n_records) AS n_records,
207	               min(sample_record) AS sample_record
208	        FROM entity_aliases GROUP BY 1, 2)
209	      SELECT kind || ':' || coalesce(senate_id, norm_key), kind, canonical_name,
210	             norm_key, senate_id, n_aliases, n_records, sample_record
211	      FROM grouped""")
212	
213	    con.execute("""
214	      UPDATE entity_aliases a SET entity_id = (
215	        SELECT e.entity_id FROM entities e
216	        WHERE e.kind = a.kind AND e.norm_key = a.norm_key)""")
217	
218	    # Engagement crosswalk on the verified compound key.

220	      INSERT INTO registrant_crosswalk
221	      WITH s AS (
222	        SELECT registrant_id, client_id,
223	               any_value(registrant_name) AS registrant_name,
224	               any_value(client_name) AS client_name,
225	               count(*) AS n_senate_filings, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_senate_uuid
226	        FROM senate_filings GROUP BY 1, 2),
227	      h AS (
228	        SELECT split_part(senate_reg_id, '-', 1) AS registrant_id,
229	               split_part(senate_reg_id, '-', 2) AS client_id,
230	               count(*) AS n_house_filings, min(filing_id) AS sample_house_filing_id
231	        FROM house_filings WHERE senate_reg_id LIKE '%-%' GROUP BY 1, 2)
232	      SELECT s.registrant_id, s.client_id, s.registrant_name, s.client_name,
233	             s.n_senate_filings, coalesce(h.n_house_filings, 0),
234	             s.sample_senate_uuid, h.sample_house_filing_id,
235	             CASE WHEN h.registrant_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'id' END
236	      FROM s LEFT JOIN h USING (registrant_id, client_id)""")

238	    # P1 canonical client spend (rollup-correctness). Depends on entities +
239	    # entity_aliases above; see CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW note for the rule.
240	    con.execute(CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW)

257	      FROM entity_aliases

274	              f"canonical ${canon:,.0f} ({(naive - canon) / naive:.1%} rollup "

276	        for name, d in con.execute(

279	                "AND has_inhouse_filing GROUP BY 1 HAVING d > 0 "

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build cross-dataset entity tables for the GAIN lobbying database.

Creates three tables and one view inside an existing loader-built DuckDB:

  entities           one row per resolved entity (registrant / client / foreign_entity)
  entity_aliases     every raw name variant -> entity, with a sample raw-record pointer
  registrant_crosswalk
                     senate registrant+client engagement -> matched house filings,
                     via the House <senateID> compound key
                     "<senate_registrant_id>-<senate_client_id>" (verified against real
                     records 2026-07-06; sampled pairs agree on names). ID matches are
                     labeled confidence='id'; there is no fuzzy tier in this pass —
                     name-only candidates are reported, never silently merged.
  v_client_canonical_spend (VIEW)
                     per-client, per-quarter senate lobbying spend with the in-house
                     rollup double-count removed (P1). See CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW below
                     for the rule; queries/p1_canonical_spend.sql for cited demos/QA.

Normalization (deterministic, in `norm_name` below): uppercase, strip whitespace,
drop parenthetical qualifiers ("MUBADALA INVESTMENT COMPANY (OWNS 24% OF CATURUS)"
-> "MUBADALA INVESTMENT COMPANY"), collapse punctuation to spaces, strip legal
suffixes (LLC/INC/LTD/...) repeatedly from the tail. The normalized key is stored on
every alias row so any grouping decision can be audited in SQL.

Senate-side entities carry their LDA numeric ids; House-side organizations (no UUIDs)
attach to senate entities only through the compound-key crosswalk, otherwise they
stay unresolved rather than fuzzy-merged (ambiguity is a report, not a merge).

Usage:
  python resolve_entities.py --db db/lda_pilot.duckdb            # build tables
  python resolve_entities.py --db db/lda_pilot.duckdb --report   # QA report only
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import sys
import tempfile

import duckdb

if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")

LEGAL_SUFFIXES = {
    "LLC", "L L C", "INC", "INCORPORATED", "LTD", "LIMITED", "LLP", "L L P",
    "LP", "L P", "PLC", "CO", "CORP", "CORPORATION", "COMPANY", "PC", "PLLC",
    "SA", "S A", "AG", "GMBH", "NV", "N V", "BV", "B V", "PTY", "USA", "US",
}


def norm_name(raw):
    """Deterministic normalization key. Documented behavior; see module docstring."""
    if not raw:
        return None
    s = raw.upper().strip()
    s = re.sub(r"\([^)]*\)", " ", s)          # parenthetical qualifiers
    s = re.sub(r"[^A-Z0-9]+", " ", s).strip()  # punctuation -> spaces
    words = s.split()
    while words and words[-1] in LEGAL_SUFFIXES:
        words.pop()
    return " ".join(words) or None


DDL = """
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE entities (
  entity_id TEXT, kind TEXT, canonical_name TEXT, norm_key TEXT,
  senate_id TEXT, n_aliases INTEGER, n_records INTEGER, sample_record TEXT);

CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE entity_aliases (
  entity_id TEXT, kind TEXT, raw_name TEXT, norm_key TEXT, dataset TEXT,
  senate_id TEXT, n_records INTEGER, sample_record TEXT);

CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE registrant_crosswalk (
  senate_registrant_id TEXT, senate_client_id TEXT,
  senate_registrant_name TEXT, senate_client_name TEXT,
  n_senate_filings INTEGER, n_house_filings INTEGER,
  sample_senate_uuid TEXT, sample_house_filing_id TEXT, confidence TEXT);
"""

# One source per (kind, dataset): raw name, senate numeric id when the dataset has
# one, and a raw-record pointer sample. Foreign entities have no LDA id -> norm key
# only.
SOURCES = {
    ("registrant", "senate"): """
      SELECT registrant_name AS raw_name, registrant_id AS senate_id,
             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
      FROM senate_filings WHERE registrant_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
    ("client", "senate"): """
      SELECT client_name AS raw_name, client_id AS senate_id,
             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
      FROM senate_filings WHERE client_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
    ("registrant", "house"): """
      SELECT organization_name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_id) AS sample_record
      FROM house_filings WHERE organization_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
    ("client", "house"): """
      SELECT client_name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_id) AS sample_record
      FROM house_filings WHERE client_name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
    ("foreign_entity", "senate"): """
      SELECT name AS raw_name, NULL AS senate_id,
             count(*) AS n_records, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_record
      FROM senate_foreign_entities WHERE name IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1, 2""",
}

ALIAS_COLS = ["entity_id", "kind", "raw_name", "norm_key", "dataset",
              "senate_id", "n_records", "sample_record"]

# P1 — canonical client spend (rollup-correctness). A client that self-files
# (registrant norm_key == client norm_key, i.e. in-house lobbying) reports its TOTAL
# lobbying under that filing, which already subsumes the income the outside firms it
# hired report for the same client. Summing every filing for a client therefore
# double-counts. Per (client, quarter): canonical = greatest(in-house amount, outside
# amount) — NEVER their sum; both components + the naive sum-all + the delta are kept
# so any figure is auditable. `amount` is the row's reported figure regardless of the
# income/expenses field (some in-house filers report under `income`, not `expenses` —
# keying on `expenses` alone zeroed out real spenders like AIPAC). Amendments are
# deduped on filing_period (latest by `posted`), the same rule as sweep_2026.sql#H1c.
# Senate-only (the completeness-primary dataset; never sum senate+house). Some clients
# roll up and some don't — has_inhouse_filing + method make the branch explicit.
# NOTE: keep this in sync with queries/p1_canonical_spend.sql's header/demos.
CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW = """
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_client_canonical_spend AS
WITH rk AS (SELECT DISTINCT raw_name, norm_key FROM entity_aliases
            WHERE kind='registrant' AND dataset='senate'),
     ck AS (SELECT DISTINCT raw_name, norm_key FROM entity_aliases
            WHERE kind='client' AND dataset='senate'),
base AS (
  SELECT f.registrant_id, f.client_id, f.filing_year, f.filing_period, f.posted,
         greatest(coalesce(f.income, 0), coalesce(f.expenses, 0)) AS amount,
         rk.norm_key AS reg_key, ck.norm_key AS cli_key
  FROM senate_filings f
  LEFT JOIN rk ON rk.raw_name = f.registrant_name
  LEFT JOIN ck ON ck.raw_name = f.client_name
  WHERE f.filing_period IS NOT NULL
    AND (f.income IS NOT NULL OR f.expenses IS NOT NULL)
    AND ck.norm_key IS NOT NULL),
dedup AS (
  SELECT * FROM base QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
    PARTITION BY registrant_id, client_id, filing_year, filing_period
    ORDER BY posted DESC) = 1),
tagged AS (
  SELECT *, (reg_key IS NOT NULL AND reg_key = cli_key) AS self_filed FROM dedup),
agg AS (
  SELECT cli_key, filing_year, filing_period,
         bool_or(self_filed) AS has_inhouse_filing,
         coalesce(sum(amount) FILTER (WHERE self_filed), 0) AS inhouse_amount,
         coalesce(sum(amount) FILTER (WHERE NOT self_filed), 0) AS outside_amount,
         count(*) AS n_filings
  FROM tagged GROUP BY cli_key, filing_year, filing_period)
SELECT e.entity_id AS client_entity_id,
       coalesce(e.canonical_name, a.cli_key) AS client_name,
       a.cli_key AS client_norm_key, a.filing_year, a.filing_period,
       a.has_inhouse_filing, a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount,
       greatest(a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount) AS canonical_spend,
       a.inhouse_amount + a.outside_amount AS naive_sum_all,
       least(a.inhouse_amount, a.outside_amount) AS double_count_delta,
       CASE WHEN a.has_inhouse_filing AND a.inhouse_amount >= a.outside_amount
              THEN 'in-house total (outside firms subsumed)'
            WHEN a.has_inhouse_filing
              THEN 'outside sum (in-house filing under-reported)'
            ELSE 'outside sum (no in-house filing)' END AS method,
       a.n_filings
FROM agg a LEFT JOIN entities e ON e.kind = 'client' AND e.norm_key = a.cli_key
"""


def build(con):
    for stmt in DDL.split(";"):
        if stmt.strip():
            con.execute(stmt)

    # Normalize in Python (no numpy/UDF dependency); stage via NDJSON + COPY,
    # same pattern as the loader's Sink.
    rows = []
    for (kind, dataset), sql in SOURCES.items():
        for raw_name, senate_id, n_records, sample in con.execute(sql).fetchall():
            key = norm_name(raw_name)
            if key:
                rows.append(dict(zip(ALIAS_COLS, (
                    None, kind, raw_name, key, dataset,
                    senate_id, n_records, sample))))
    fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".ndjson")
    os.close(fd)
    try:
        with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            for r in rows:
                f.write(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False, default=str) + "\n")
        con.execute(
            f"COPY entity_aliases FROM '{tmp.replace(chr(92), '/')}' (FORMAT json)")
    finally:
        os.unlink(tmp)

    # Entity id: senate numeric id where any alias in the (kind, norm_key) group
    # carries one (senate ids are authoritative), else the norm key. Kind is part of
    # the id so a firm that is both registrant and client stays two entities (they
    # play different roles; the crosswalk join is what links engagements).
    con.execute("""
      INSERT INTO entities
      WITH grouped AS (
        SELECT kind, norm_key, min(senate_id) AS senate_id,
               arg_max(raw_name, n_records) AS canonical_name,
               count(*) AS n_aliases, sum(n_records) AS n_records,
               min(sample_record) AS sample_record
        FROM entity_aliases GROUP BY 1, 2)
      SELECT kind || ':' || coalesce(senate_id, norm_key), kind, canonical_name,
             norm_key, senate_id, n_aliases, n_records, sample_record
      FROM grouped""")

    con.execute("""
      UPDATE entity_aliases a SET entity_id = (
        SELECT e.entity_id FROM entities e
        WHERE e.kind = a.kind AND e.norm_key = a.norm_key)""")

    # Engagement crosswalk on the verified compound key.
    con.execute("""
      INSERT INTO registrant_crosswalk
      WITH s AS (
        SELECT registrant_id, client_id,
               any_value(registrant_name) AS registrant_name,
               any_value(client_name) AS client_name,
               count(*) AS n_senate_filings, min(filing_uuid) AS sample_senate_uuid
        FROM senate_filings GROUP BY 1, 2),
      h AS (
        SELECT split_part(senate_reg_id, '-', 1) AS registrant_id,
               split_part(senate_reg_id, '-', 2) AS client_id,
               count(*) AS n_house_filings, min(filing_id) AS sample_house_filing_id
        FROM house_filings WHERE senate_reg_id LIKE '%-%' GROUP BY 1, 2)
      SELECT s.registrant_id, s.client_id, s.registrant_name, s.client_name,
             s.n_senate_filings, coalesce(h.n_house_filings, 0),
             s.sample_senate_uuid, h.sample_house_filing_id,
             CASE WHEN h.registrant_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'id' END
      FROM s LEFT JOIN h USING (registrant_id, client_id)""")

    # P1 canonical client spend (rollup-correctness). Depends on entities +
    # entity_aliases above; see CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW note for the rule.
    con.execute(CANONICAL_SPEND_VIEW)


def report(con):
    print("# Entity crosswalk QA report\n")
    for kind, in con.execute("SELECT DISTINCT kind FROM entities ORDER BY 1").fetchall():
        n_e, n_a = con.execute(
            "SELECT count(*), sum(n_aliases) FROM entities WHERE kind=?", [kind]).fetchone()
        print(f"{kind}: {n_e:,} entities from {n_a:,} raw name variants")
    total, matched = con.execute("""
      SELECT count(*), count(sample_house_filing_id) FROM registrant_crosswalk""").fetchone()
    print(f"\nengagements (senate registrant+client pairs): {total:,}; "
          f"matched to house via compound senateID: {matched:,} ({matched/total:.1%})")
    print("\nAmbiguous clusters (same norm key, >1 senate id — NOT merged, listed for audit):")
    rows = con.execute("""
      SELECT norm_key, count(DISTINCT senate_id) AS n_ids,
             string_agg(DISTINCT senate_id, ', ') AS ids
      FROM entity_aliases
      WHERE senate_id IS NOT NULL
      GROUP BY norm_key HAVING count(DISTINCT senate_id) > 1
      ORDER BY n_ids DESC LIMIT 15""").fetchall()
    for r in rows:
        print(f"  {r[0][:60]:60s} ids: {r[2][:80]}")
    if not rows:
        print("  none")

    # P1 canonical-spend sanity: how much naive client-level summing overstates.
    try:
        yr = con.execute(
            "SELECT max(filing_year) FROM v_client_canonical_spend").fetchone()[0]
        naive, canon = con.execute(
            "SELECT sum(naive_sum_all), sum(canonical_spend) "
            "FROM v_client_canonical_spend WHERE filing_year = ?", [yr]).fetchone()
        print(f"\nCanonical client spend (P1), {yr}: naive sum-all ${naive:,.0f} vs "
              f"canonical ${canon:,.0f} ({(naive - canon) / naive:.1%} rollup "
              f"double-count removed). Top in-house rollups by amount avoided:")
        for name, d in con.execute(
                "SELECT client_name, sum(double_count_delta) d "
                "FROM v_client_canonical_spend WHERE filing_year = ? "
                "AND has_inhouse_filing GROUP BY 1 HAVING d > 0 "
                "ORDER BY d DESC LIMIT 5", [yr]).fetchall():
            print(f"  {name[:48]:48s} ${d:,.0f}")
    except duckdb.Error:
        print("\n(v_client_canonical_spend not present — rebuild with build())")


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--db", default="db/lda_pilot.duckdb")
    ap.add_argument("--report", action="store_true",
                    help="QA report only (tables must already exist)")
    args = ap.parse_args()
    con = duckdb.connect(args.db, read_only=args.report)
    if not args.report:
        build(con)
    report(con)
    con.close()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
1	-- P2 bill cross-check — the citeable aggregate form of skills/lead-scanner/scripts/bill_lookup.py.
2	-- The script is the ergonomic front door (number OR alias, all datasets, sample keys);
3	-- these labeled blocks are what an aggregate claim cites, per CLAUDE.md's "aggregate
4	-- claims cite the exact SQL block + the one-command rebuild + >=3 sampled records" rule.
5	--
6	-- Run:  .venv/Scripts/python queries/run_sweep.py db/lda_full.duckdb P2 queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql
7	-- Demo bill = HR5376 (Inflation Reduction Act). To cross-check a different bill, swap the
8	-- two literals: the normalized number ('HR5376', per build_db.py norm_bill) and, for the
9	-- name side, the alias's precise phrase ('inflation reduction act') from bill_aliases.json.
10	-- For a PHRASE-PRIMARY bill (Farm Bill: no reliable number — H.R.2 is reused every Congress),
11	-- use P2d, which name-matches filing free-text instead of the number.
12	--
13	-- Discipline baked in (same as CLAUDE.md data-facts): senate is amendment/duplicate-deduped
14	-- on filing_period (NOT filing_type); senate and house are reported SEPARATELY, never summed
15	-- (LD-2s are filed with both chambers); attributed income is FILING-LEVEL (a filing naming
16	-- several bills attributes its full income to each) — a ranking signal, not exact dollars.
17	
18	-- ==== P2a: cross-dataset touch counts for HR5376 (who cites the number vs names it) ====
19	WITH num AS (
20	  SELECT dataset, count(DISTINCT record_key) AS filings_or_releases
21	  FROM bill_mentions WHERE bill = 'HR5376' GROUP BY 1),
22	name_press AS (
23	  SELECT count(*) AS n FROM press_releases
24	  WHERE regexp_matches(text, '(?i)\binflation reduction act\b'))
25	SELECT 'senate: cite number' AS signal,
26	       (SELECT filings_or_releases FROM num WHERE dataset = 'senate') AS n
27	UNION ALL SELECT 'house: cite number',
28	       (SELECT filings_or_releases FROM num WHERE dataset = 'house')
29	UNION ALL SELECT 'press: cite number',
30	       (SELECT coalesce(filings_or_releases, 0) FROM num WHERE dataset = 'press')
31	UNION ALL SELECT 'press: NAME the bill (say-vs-pay bridge)', (SELECT n FROM name_press);
32	
33	-- ==== P2b: senate top clients behind HR5376 — deduped engagement-quarters, ranked ====
34	WITH keys AS (
35	  SELECT DISTINCT record_key FROM bill_mentions
36	  WHERE dataset = 'senate' AND bill = 'HR5376'),
37	f AS (
38	  SELECT sf.filing_uuid, sf.registrant_name, sf.client_name, sf.income,
39	         sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period
40	  FROM senate_filings sf JOIN keys ON sf.filing_uuid = keys.record_key
41	  QUALIFY row_number() OVER (
42	    PARTITION BY upper(trim(sf.registrant_name)), upper(trim(sf.client_name)),
43	                 sf.filing_year, sf.filing_period
44	    ORDER BY sf.posted DESC) = 1)
45	SELECT client_name, sum(income)::BIGINT AS attributed_income, count(*) AS filings
46	FROM f GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY attributed_income DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 15;
47	
48	-- ==== P2c: press say-vs-pay for HR5376 — number-cited vs name-only ====
49	-- The name-only count is the coverage a number-only match (the L004 trap) would miss.
50	WITH num AS (
51	  SELECT DISTINCT src_file || ':' || src_line AS k FROM press_releases pr
52	  JOIN bill_mentions bm ON bm.record_key = pr.src_file || ':' || pr.src_line
53	  WHERE bm.dataset = 'press' AND bm.bill = 'HR5376'),
54	nm AS (
55	  SELECT DISTINCT src_file || ':' || src_line AS k FROM press_releases
56	  WHERE regexp_matches(text, '(?i)\binflation reduction act\b'))
57	SELECT (SELECT count(*) FROM num) AS cite_number,
58	       (SELECT count(*) FROM nm) AS name_it,
59	       (SELECT count(*) FROM nm WHERE k NOT IN (SELECT k FROM num)) AS name_only;
60	
61	-- ==== P2d: phrase-primary path (Farm Bill) — name-matched filing free-text ====
62	-- No reliable number (H.R.2 is reassigned each Congress), so the filing-side signal is the
63	-- filers' own free-text (senate activity descriptions, house specific_issues). All rows keep
64	-- a show_record.py-resolvable key (senate filing_uuid / house filing_id).
65	-- Phrase set mirrors bill_aliases.json farm-bill.phrases so counts match bill_lookup.py.
66	SELECT 'senate: activity text names it' AS signal,
67	       count(DISTINCT filing_uuid) AS filings
68	FROM senate_activities
69	WHERE regexp_matches(description, '(?i)\b(farm bill|agriculture improvement act)\b')
70	UNION ALL
71	SELECT 'house: specific_issues names it', count(DISTINCT filing_id)
72	FROM house_filings
73	WHERE regexp_matches(specific_issues, '(?i)\b(farm bill|agriculture improvement act)\b')
74	UNION ALL
75	SELECT 'press: names it', count(*)
76	FROM press_releases
77	WHERE regexp_matches(text, '(?i)\b(farm bill|agriculture improvement act)\b');
78
